units.text: Enter RUMOR all painted with tongues

units.speaker: RUMOR
units.text:
Open your ears, for which of you will stop The vent of
hearing when loud Rumor speaks? I, from the orient to
the drooping west, Making the wind my post-horse, still
unfold The acts commenced on this ball of earth. Upon my
tongues continual slanders ride, The which in every
language I pronounce, Stuffing the ears of men with
false reports. I speak of peace while covert enmity
Under the smile of safety wounds the world. And who but
Rumor, who but only I, Make fearful musters and prepared
defense, Whiles the big year, swoll'n with some other
grief, Is thought with child by the stern tyrant war,
And no such matter? Rumor is a pipe Blown by surmises,
jealousies, conjectures, And of so easy and so plain a
stop That the blunt monster with uncounted heads, The
still-discordant wav'ring multitude, Can play upon it.
But what need I thus My well-known body to anatomize
Among my household? Why is Rumor here? I run before King
Harry’s victory, Who in a bloody field by Shrewsbury
Hath beaten down young Hotspur and his troops, Quenching
the flame of bold rebellion Even with the rebels' blood.
But what mean I To speak so true at first? My office is
To noise abroad that Harry Monmouth fell Under the
wrath of noble Hotspur’s sword, And that the King before
the Douglas' rage Stooped his anointed head as low as
death. This have I rumored through the peasant towns
Between that royal field of Shrewsbury And this
worm-eaten hold of ragged stone, Where Hotspur’s father,
old Northumberland, Lies crafty-sick. The posts come
tiring on, And not a man of them brings other news Than
they have learnt of me. From Rumor’s tongues They bring
smooth comforts false, worse than true wrongs.
Exit
Enter LORD BARDOLPH

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text: Who keeps the gate here, ho?
Enter the PORTER

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text: Where is the Earl?

units.speaker: PORTER
units.text: What shall I say you are?

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
Tell thou the Earl That the Lord Bardolph doth
attend him here.

units.speaker: PORTER
units.text:
His lordship is walked forth into the orchard. Please
it your Honor knock but at the gate And he himself will
answer.
NORTHUMBERLAND Enter

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text: Here comes the Earl.
Exit PORTER

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
What news, Lord Bardolph? Every minute now Should be
the father of some stratagem. The times are wild.
Contention, like a horse Full of high feeding, madly hath
broke loose And bears down all before him.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
Noble Earl, I bring you certain news from
Shrewsbury.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text: Good, an God will!

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
As good as heart can wish. The King is almost wounded
to the death, And, in the fortune of my lord your son,
Prince Harry slain outright; and both the Blunts Killed
by the hand of Douglas; young Prince John And
Westmoreland and Stafford fled the field; And Harry
Monmouth’s brawn, the hulk Sir John, Is prisoner to your
son. O, such a day, So fought, so followed, and so
fairly won, Came not till now to dignify the times Since
Caesar’s fortunes.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
How is this derived? Saw you the field? Came you
from Shrewsbury?

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
I spake with one, my lord, that came from thence, A
gentleman well bred and of good name, That freely
rendered me these news for true.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Here comes my servant Travers, who I sent On Tuesday
last to listen after news.
Enter TRAVERS

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
My lord, I overrode him on the way; And he is furnished
with no certainties More than he haply may retail from
me.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text: Now, Travers, what good tidings comes with you?

units.speaker: TRAVERS
units.text:
My lord, Sir John Umfrevile turned me back With joyful
tidings and, being better horsed, Outrode me. After him
came spurring hard A gentleman, almost forspent with
speed, That stopp’d by me to breathe his bloodied horse.
He asked the way to Chester, and of him I did demand
what news from Shrewsbury. He told me that rebellion had
bad luck And that young Harry Percy’s spur was cold.
With that he gave his able horse the head And, bending
forward, struck his armèd heels Against the panting
sides of his poor jade Up to the rowel-head, and
starting so He seemed in running to devour the way,
Staying no longer question.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Ha? Again: Said he young Harry Percy’s spur was
cold? Of Hotspur, Coldspur? That rebellion Had met ill
luck?

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
My lord, I’ll tell you what: If my young lord your
son have not the day, Upon mine honor, for a silken
point I’ll give my barony. Never talk of it.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Why should that gentleman that rode by Travers Give
then such instances of loss?

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
Who, he? He was some hilding fellow that had stolen
The horse he rode on and, upon my life, Spoke at a
venture. Look, here comes more news.
Enter MORTON

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Yea, this man’s brow, like to a title leaf, Foretells
the nature of a tragic volume. So looks the strond
whereon the imperious flood Hath left a witness’d
usurpation. — Say, Morton, didst thou come from
Shrewsbury?

units.speaker: MORTON
units.text:
I ran from Shrewsbury, my noble lord, Where hateful
death put on his ugliest mask To fright our party.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
How doth my son and brother? Thou tremblest, and the
whiteness in thy cheek Is apter than thy tongue to tell
thy errand. Even such a man, so faint, so spiritless, So
dull, so dead in look, so woebegone, Drew Priam’s
curtain in the dead of night, And would have told him
half his Troy was burnt; But Priam found the fire ere he
his tongue, And I my Percy’s death ere thou report’st
it. This thou wouldst say, “Your son did thus and thus;
Your brother thus; so fought the noble Douglas”— Stopping
my greedy ear with their bold deeds. But in the end, to
stop my ear indeed, Thou hast a sigh to blow away this
praise, Ending with “Brother, son, and all are dead.”

units.speaker: MORTON
units.text:
Douglas is living, and your brother yet, But for my
lord your son—

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Why, he is dead. See what a ready tongue suspicion
hath! He that but fears the thing he would not know
Hath, by instinct, knowledge from others' eyes That what
he feared is chancèd . Yet speak, Morton. Tell thou an
earl his divination lies, And I will take it as a sweet
disgrace And make thee rich for doing me such wrong.

units.speaker: MORTON
units.text:
You are too great to be by me gainsaid, Your spirit is
too true, your fears too certain.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Yet, for all this, say not that Percy’s dead. I see a
strange confession in thine eye. Thou shak’st thy head
and hold’st it fear or sin To speak a truth. If he be
slain, say so. The tongue offends not that reports his
death; And he doth sin that doth belie the dead, Not he
which says the dead is not alive. Yet the first bringer
of unwelcome news Hath but a losing office, and his
tongue Sounds ever after as a sullen bell Remembered
tolling a departing friend.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text: I cannot think, my lord, your son is dead.

units.speaker: MORTON
units.text:
I am sorry I should force you to believe That which I
would to God I had not seen, But these mine eyes saw him
in bloody state, Rend'ring faint quittance, wearied and
outbreathed, To Harry Monmouth; whose swift wrath beat
down The never-daunted Percy to the earth, From whence
with life he never more sprung up. In few, his death,
whose spirit lent a fire Even to the dullest peasant in
his camp, Being bruited once, took fire and heat away
From the best tempered courage in his troops; For from
his metal was his party steeled, Which, once in him
abated, all the rest Turned on themselves, like dull and
heavy lead. And as the thing that’s heavy in itself
Upon enforcement flies with greatest speed, So did our
men, heavy in Hotspur’s loss, Lend to this weight such
lightness with their fear That arrows fled not swifter
toward their aim Than did our soldiers, aiming at their
safety, Fly from the field. Then was the noble Worcester
Too soon ta'en prisoner; and that furious Scot, The
bloody Douglas, whose well-laboring sword Had three
times slain th'appearance of the King, Gan vail his
stomach and did grace the shame Of those that turned
their backs and in his flight, Stumbling in fear, was
took. The sum of all Is that the King hath won and hath
sent out A speedy power to encounter you, my lord, Under
the conduct of young Lancaster And Westmoreland. This
is the news at full.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
For this I shall have time enough to mourn. In poison
there is physic, and these news, Having been well, that
would have made me sick, Being sick, have in some
measure made me well. And as the wretch whose
fever-weakened joints, Like strengthless hinges, buckle
under life, Impatient of his fit, breaks like a fire Out
of his keeper’s arms, even so my limbs, Weakened with
grief, being now enraged with grief, Are thrice
themselves. Hence therefore, thou nice crutch. A scaly
gauntlet now with joints of steel Must glove this hand.
And hence, thou sickly coif. Thou art a guard too wanton
for the head Which princes, fleshed with conquest, aim
to hit. Now bind my brows with iron, and approach The
ragged’st hour that time and spite dare bring To frown
upon th'enraged Northumberland. Let heaven kiss earth!
Now let not Nature’s hand Keep the wild flood confined.
Let order die, And let this world no longer be a stage
To feed contention in a lingering act; But let one spirit
of the firstborn Cain Reign in all bosoms, that, each
heart being set On bloody courses, the rude scene may
end, And darkness be the burier of the dead.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text: This strainèd passion doth you wrong, my lord.

units.speaker: MORTON
units.text:
Sweet Earl, divorce not wisdom from your honor. The
lives of all your loving complices Lean on your health,
the which, if you give o'er To stormy passion, must
perforce decay. You cast th' event of war, my noble
lord, And summed the account of chance before you said
“Let us make head.” It was your presurmise That, in the
dole of blows your son might drop. You knew he walked
o'er perils on an edge, More likely to fall in than to
get o'er. You were advised his flesh was capable Of
wounds and scars, and that his forward spirit Would lift
him where most trade of danger ranged. Yet did you say
“Go forth,” and none of this, Though strongly
apprehended, could restrain The stiff-borne action. What
hath then befall'n, Or what did this bold enterprise
brought forth, More than that being which was like to
be?

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
We all that are engagèd to this loss Knew that we
ventured on such dangerous seas That if we wrought out
life, ’twas ten to one; And yet we ventured, for the
gain proposed Choked the respect of likely peril feared;
And since we are o'erset, venture again. Come, we will
all put forth, body and goods.

units.speaker: MORTON
units.text:
'Tis more than time.—And, my most noble lord, I hear
for certain, and do speak the truth: The gentle
Archbishop of York is up With well-appointed powers. He
is a man Who with a double surety binds his followers.
My lord your son had only but the corpse, But shadows and
the shows of men, to fight; For that same word
“rebellion” did divide The action of their bodies from
their souls, And they did fight with queasiness,
constrained, As men drink potions, that their weapons
only Seemed on our side. But, for their spirits and
souls, This word “rebellion,” it had froze them up As
fish are in a pond. But now the Bishop Turns
insurrection to religion. Supposed sincere and holy in
his thoughts, He’s followed both with body and with
mind, And doth enlarge his rising with the blood Of fair
King Richard, scraped from Pomfret stones; Derives from
heaven his quarrel and his cause; Tells them he doth
bestride a bleeding land, Gasping for life under great
Bolingbroke; And more and less do flock to follow him.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
I knew of this before, but, to speak truth, This
present grief had wiped it from my mind. Go in with me
and counsel every man The aptest way for safety and
revenge. Get posts and letters, and make friends with
speed. Never so few, and never yet more need.
Exeunt
Enter Sir John FALSTAFF, with his PAGE bearing his sword and buckler

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Sirrah, you giant, what says the doctor to my water?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
He said, sir, the water itself was a good healthy
water, but, for the party that owed it, he might have
more diseases than he knew for.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Men of all sorts take a pride to gird at me. The brain
of this foolish-compounded clay, man, is not able to
invent anything that tends to laughter more than I
invent, or is invented on me. I am not only witty in
myself, but the cause that wit is in other men. I do
here walk before thee like a sow that hath overwhelmed
all her litter but one. If the Prince put thee into my
service for any other reason than to set me off, why
then I have no judgment. Thou whoreson mandrake, thou
art fitter to be worn in my cap than to wait at my
heels. I was never manned with an agate till now, but I
will inset you neither in gold nor silver, but in vile
apparel, and send you back again to your master for a
jewel. The juvenal, the Prince your master, whose chin
is not yet fledge—I will sooner have a beard grow in the
palm of my hand than he shall get one off his cheek,
and yet he will not stick to say his face is a face
royal. God may finish it when He will. 'Tis not a hair
amiss yet. He may keep it still at a face royal, for a
barber shall never earn sixpence out of it, and yet
he’ll be crowing as if he had writ man ever since his
father was a bachelor. He may keep his own grace, but
he’s almost out of mine, I can assure him. What said
Master Dommelton about the satin for my short cloak and
my slops?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
He said, sir, you should procure him better assurance
than Bardolph. He would not take his band and yours. He
liked not the security.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Let him be damned like the glutton! Pray God his tongue
be hotter! A whoreson Achitophel, a rascally
yea-forsooth knave, to bear a gentleman in hand and then
stand upon security! The whoreson smoothy-pates do now
wear nothing but high shoes and bunches of keys at their
girdles; and if a man is through with them in honest
taking up, then they must stand upon security. I had as
lief they would put ratsbane in my mouth as offer to
stop it with “security.” I looked he should have sent me
two-and-twenty yards of satin, as I am a true knight,
and he sends me “security.” Well, he may sleep in
security, for he hath the horn of abundance, and the
lightness of his wife shines through it, and yet cannot
he see though he have his own lantern to light him.
Where’s Bardolph?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: He’s gone into Smithfield to buy your Worship a horse.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I bought him in Paul’s, and he’ll buy me a horse in
Smithfield. An I could get me but a wife in the stews, I
were manned, horsed, and wived.
Enter the Lord CHIEF JUSTICE and SERVANT

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Sir, here comes the nobleman that committed the Prince
for striking him about Bardolph.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Wait close. I will not see him.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: What’s he that goes there?

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: Falstaff, an ’t please your Lordship.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: He that was in question for the robbery?

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text:
He, my lord; but he hath since done good service at
Shrewsbury, and, as I hear, is now going with some
charge to the Lord John of Lancaster.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: What, to York? Call him back again.

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: Sir John Falstaff!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Boy, tell him I am deaf.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: You must speak louder. My master is deaf.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
I am sure he is, to the hearing of any thing good.—Go
pluck him by the elbow. I must speak with him.

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: Sir John!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What, a young knave and begging? Is there not wars? Is
there not employment? Doth not the King lack subjects? Do
not the rebels need soldiers? Though it be a shame to
be on any side but one, it is worse shame to beg than to
be on the worst side, were it worse than the name of
rebellion can tell how to make it.

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: You mistake me, sir.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Why sir, did I say you were an honest man? Setting my
knighthood and my soldiership aside, I had lied in my
throat if I had said so.

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text:
I pray you, sir, then set your knighthood and our
soldiership aside, and give me leave to tell you, you
lie in your throat if you say I am any other than an
honest man.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I give thee leave to tell me so? I lay aside that which
grows to me? If thou gett’st any leave of me, hang me;
if thou tak’st leave, thou wert better be hanged. You
hunt counter. Hence! Avaunt!

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: Sir, my lord would speak with you.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Sir John Falstaff, a word with you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
My good lord. God give your Lordship good time of the
day. I am glad to see your Lordship abroad. I heard say
your Lordship was sick: I hope your Lordship goes abroad
by advice. Your Lordship, though not clean past your
youth, have yet some smack of an age in you, some relish
of the saltness of time in you, and I most humbly
beseech your Lordship to have a reverent care of your
health.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Sir John, I sent for you before your expedition to
Shrewsbury.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
An ’t please your Lordship, I hear his Majesty is
returned with some discomfort from Wales.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
I talk not of his Majesty. You would not come when I
sent for you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
And I hear, moreover, his Highness is fallen into this
same whoreson apoplexy.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Well, God mend him. I pray you let me speak with you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
This apoplexy, as I take it, is a kind of lethargy , an
’t please your Lordship, a kind of sleeping in the
blood, a whoreson tingling.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: What tell you me of it? Be it as it is.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
It hath its original from much grief, from study, and
perturbation of the brain. I have read the cause of his
effects in Galen. It is a kind of deafness.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
I think you are fallen into the disease, for you hear
not what I say to you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Very well, my lord, very well. Rather, an ’t please
you, it is the disease of not listening, the malady of
not marking, that I am troubled withal.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
To punish you by the heels would amend the attention of
your ears, and I care not if I do become your
physician.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I am as poor as Job, my lord, but not so patient. Your
Lordship may minister the potion of imprisonment to me in
respect of poverty, but how should I be your patient to
follow your prescriptions, the wise may make some dram
of a scruple, or indeed a scruple itself.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
I sent for you, when there were matters against you for
your life, to come speak with me.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
As I was then advised by my learned counsel in the laws
of this land-service, I did not come.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Well, the truth is, Sir John, you live in great infamy.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: He that buckles him in my belt cannot live in less.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Your means are very slender, and your waste is great.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I would it were otherwise. I would my means were
greater and my waist slender.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: You have misled the youthful Prince.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
The young Prince hath misled me. I am the fellow with
the great belly, and he my dog.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Well, I am loath to gall a new-healed wound. Your day’s
service at Shrewsbury hath a little gilded over your
night’s exploit on Gad’s Hill. You may thank th' unquiet
time for your quiet o'erposting that action.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: My lord!

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
But since all is well, keep it so. Wake not a sleeping
wolf.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: To wake a wolf is as bad as to smell a fox.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: What, you are as a candle, the better part burnt out.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A wassail candle, my lord, all tallow. If I did say of
wax, my growth would approve the truth.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
There is not a white hair on your face but should have
his effect of gravity.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: His effect of gravy, gravy, gravy.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
You follow the young Prince up and down like his ill
angel.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Not so, my lord. Your ill angel is light, but I hope he
that looks upon me will take me without weighing. And
yet in some respects I grant I cannot go. I cannot tell.
Virtue is of so little regard in these costermongers'
times that true valor is turned bear-herd; pregnancy is
made a tapster, and hath his quick wit wasted in giving
reckonings. All the other gifts appurtenant to man, as
the malice of this age shapes them, are not worth a
gooseberry. You that are old consider not the capacities
of us that are young. You do measure the heat of our
livers with the bitterness of your galls , and we that
are in the vaward of our youth, I must confess, are wags
too.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Do you set down your name in the scroll of youth, that
are written down old with all the characters of age?
Have you not a moist eye, a dry hand, a yellow cheek, a
white beard, a decreasing leg, an increasing belly? Is
not your voice broken, your wind short, your chin
double, your wit single, and every part about you
blasted with antiquity? And will you yet call yourself
young? Fie, fie, fie, Sir John

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
My lord, I was born about three of the clock in the
afternoon, with a white head and something a round
belly. For my voice, I have lost it with halloing and
singing of anthems. To approve my youth further, I will
not. The truth is, I am only old in judgment and
understanding. And he that will caper with me for a
thousand marks, let him lend me the money, and have at
him! For the box of the ear that the Prince gave you, he
gave it like a rude prince, and you took it like a
sensible lord. I have checked him for it, and the young
lion repents. Marry, not in ashes and sackcloth, but in
new silk and old sack.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Well, God send the Prince a better companion.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
God send the companion a better prince. I cannot rid my
hands of him.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Well, the King hath severed you and Prince Harry. I
hear you are going with Lord John of Lancaster against
the Archbishop and the Earl of Northumberland.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Yea, I thank your pretty sweet wit for it. But look you
pray, all you that kiss my Lady Peace at home, that our
armies join not in a hot day, for, by the Lord, I take
but two shirts out with me, and I mean not to sweat
extraordinarily. If it be a hot day and I brandish
anything but a bottle, I would I might never spit white
again. There is not a dangerous action can peep out his
head but I am thrust upon it. Well, I cannot last ever.
But it was always yet the trick of our English nation,
if they have a good thing, to make it too common. If ye
will needs say I am an old man, you should give me rest.
I would to God my name were not so terrible to the
enemy as it is. I were better to be eaten to death with
a rust than to be scoured to nothing with perpetual
motion.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Well, be honest, be honest; and God bless your
expedition!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Will your Lordship lend me a thousand pound to furnish
me forth?

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Not a penny, not a penny. You are too impatient to bear
crosses. Fare you well. Commend me to my cousin
Westmoreland.
Exeunt CHIEF JUSTICE and SERVANT

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
If I do, fillip me with a three-man beetle. A man can
no more separate age and covetousness than he can part
young limbs and lechery; but the gout galls the one, and
the pox pinches the other, and so both the degrees
prevent my curses. —Boy!

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Sir.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: What money is in my purse?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Seven groats and two pence.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I can get no remedy against this consumption of the
purse. Borrowing only lingers and lingers it out, but
the disease is incurable. Go bear this letter to my Lord
of Lancaster, this to the Prince, this to the Earl of
Westmoreland; and this to old Mistress Ursula, whom I
have weekly sworn to marry since I perceived the first
white hair on my chin . About it. You know where to find
me.
Exit PAGE

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A pox of this gout! Or, a gout of this pox, for the one
or the other plays the rogue with my great toe. 'Tis no
matter if I do halt. I have the wars for my color, and
my pension shall seem the more reasonable. A good wit
will make use of anything. I will turn diseases to
commodity.
Exit
Enter the ARCHBISHOP of York, Thomas MOWBRAY the Earl Marshal, Lord HASTINGS, and LORD BARDOLPH

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text:
Thus have you heard our cause and known our means, And,
my most noble friends, I pray you all Speak plainly
your opinions of our hopes. And first, Lord Marshal,
what say you to it?

units.speaker: MOWBRAY
units.text:
I well allow the occasion of our arms, But gladly would
be better satisfied How in our means we should advance
ourselves To look with forehead bold and big enough Upon
the power and puissance of the King.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
Our present musters grow upon the file To
five-and-twenty thousand men of choice, And our supplies
live largely in the hope Of great Northumberland, whose
bosom burns With an incensèd fire of injuries.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
The question then, Lord Hastings, standeth thus:
Whether our present five-and-twenty thousand May hold up
head without Northumberland.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: With him we may.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
Yea, marry, there’s the point. But if without him we
be thought too feeble, My judgment is we should not
step too far Till we had his assistance by the hand. For
in a theme so bloody-faced as this, Conjecture,
expectation, and surmise Of aids incertain should not be
admitted.

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text:
'Tis very true, Lord Bardolph; for indeed It was young
Hotspur’s cause at Shrewsbury.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
It was, my lord; who lined himself with hope, Eating
the air on promise of supply, Flatt'ring himself in
project of a power Much smaller than the smallest of his
thoughts, And so, with great imagination Proper to
madmen, led his powers to death And, winking, leapt into
destruction.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
But, by your leave, it never yet did hurt To lay down
likelihoods and forms of hope.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text:
Yes, if this present quality of war— Indeed the instant
action, a cause on foot— Lives so in hope, as in an
early spring We see the appearing buds, which to prove
fruit Hope gives not so much warrant as despair That
frosts will bite them. When we mean to build, We first
survey the plot, then draw the model, And when we see
the figure of the house, Then must we rate the cost of
the erection, Which if we find outweighs ability, What
do we then but draw anew the model In fewer offices, or
at last desist To build at all? Much more in this great
work, Which is almost to pluck a kingdom down And set
another up, should we survey The plot of situation and
the model, Consent upon a sure foundation, Question
surveyors, know our own estate, How able such a work to
undergo, To weigh against his opposite. Or else We
fortify in paper and in figures, Using the names of men
instead of men, Like one that draws the model of a house
Beyond his power to build it , who, half through, Gives
o'er and leaves his part-created cost A naked subject to
the weeping clouds And waste for churlish winter’s
tyranny.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
Grant that our hopes, yet likely of fair birth, Should
be stillborn and that we now possessed The utmost man of
expectation, I think we are a body strong enough, Even
as we are, to equal with the King.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text: What, is the King but five-and twenty-thousand?

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
To us no more, nay, not so much, Lord Bardolph, For his
divisions, as the times do brawl, Are in three heads:
one power against the French, And one against Glendower;
perforce a third Must take up us. So is the unfirm King
In three divided, and his coffers sound With hollow
poverty and emptiness.

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text:
That he should draw his several strengths together And
come against us in full puissance Need not be dreaded.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
If he should do so, He leaves his back unarmed, the
French and Welsh Baying him at the heels. Never fear
that.

units.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
units.text: Who is it like should lead his forces hither?

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
The Duke of Lancaster and Westmoreland; Against the
Welsh, himself and Harry Monmouth; But who is
substituted against the French I have no certain notice.

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text:
Let us on, And publish the occasion of our arms. The
commonwealth is sick of their own choice. Their
over-greedy love hath surfeited. An habitation giddy and
unsure Hath he that buildeth on the vulgar heart. O
thou fond many, with what loud applause Didst thou beat
heaven with blessing Bolingbroke Before he was what thou
wouldst have him be. And being now trimmed in thine own
desires, Thou, beastly feeder, art so full of him That
thou provok’st thyself to cast him up. So, so, thou
common dog, didst thou disgorge Thy glutton bosom of the
royal Richard, And now thou wouldst eat thy dead vomit
up And howl’st to find it. What trust is in these times?
They that, when Richard lived, would have him die Are
now become enamored on his grave. Thou, that threw’st
dust upon his goodly head When through proud London he
came sighing on After th' admired heels of Bolingbroke,
Criest now “O earth, yield us that King again, And take
thou this!” O thoughts of men accursed! Past and to come
seems best; things present, worst.

units.speaker: MOWBRAY
units.text: Shall we go draw our numbers and set on?

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: We are time’s subjects, and time bids begone.
Exeunt
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY, with two officers; FANG with her and SNARE following

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Master Fang, have you entered the action?

units.speaker: FANG
units.text: It is entered.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Where’s your yeoman? Is ’t a lusty yeoman? Will a'
stand to ’t?

units.speaker: FANG
units.text: Sirrah! Where’s Snare?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: O Lord, ay, good Master Snare.

units.speaker: SNARE
units.text: Here, here.

units.speaker: FANG
units.text: Snare, we must arrest Sir John Falstaff.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Yea, good Master Snare, I have entered him and all.

units.speaker: SNARE
units.text:
It may chance cost some of us our lives, for he will
stab.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Alas the day, take heed of him. He stabbed me in mine
own house, and that most beastly, in good faith. He
cares not what mischief he does. If his weapon be out,
he will foin like any devil. He will spare neither man,
woman, nor child.

units.speaker: FANG
units.text: If I can close with him, I care not for his thrust.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: No, nor I neither. I’ll be at your elbow.

units.speaker: FANG
units.text: An I but fist him once, an he come but within my vue—

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
I am undone by his going. I warrant you, he’s an
infinitive thing upon my score. Good Master Fang, hold
him sure. Good Master Snare, let him not ’scape. He
comes continuantly to Pie Corner, saving your manhoods,
to buy a saddle, and he is indited to dinner to the
Lubber’s Head in Lumbert Street, to Master Smooth’s the
silkman. I pray you, since my exion is entered, and my
case so openly known to the world, let him be brought in
to his answer. A hundred mark is a long one for a poor
lone woman to bear, and I have borne, and borne, and
borne, and have been fubbed off, and fubbed off, and
fubbed off from this day to that day, that it is a shame
to be thought on. There is no honesty in such dealing,
unless a woman should be made an ass and a beast to bear
every knave’s wrong. Yonder he comes, and that errant
malmsey-nose knave, Bardolph, with him. Do your offices,
do your offices, Master Fang and Master Snare, do me,
do me, do me your offices.
Enter FALSTAFF, BARDOLPH, and PAGE

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: How now! Whose mare’s dead? What’s the matter?

units.speaker: FANG
units.text: Sir John, I arrest you at the suit of Mistress Quickly.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Away, varlets!—Draw, Bardolph. Cut me off the villain’s
head. Throw the quean in the channel.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Throw me in the channel? I’ll throw thee in the
channel. Wilt thou, wilt thou, thou bastardly rogue?
—Murder, murder! — Ah, thou honeysuckle villain, wilt thou
kill God’s officers and the King’s? Ah, thou honeyseed
rogue, thou art a honeyseed, a man-queller, and a
woman-queller.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Keep them off, Bardolph.

units.speaker: FANG
units.text: A rescue, a rescue!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Good people, bring a rescue or two. — [to FALSTAFF ] Thou
wot, wot thou? Thou wot, wot ta? Do, do, thou rogue.
Do, thou hempseed.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Away, you scullion, you rampallion, you fustilarian!
I’ll tickle your catastrophe.
Enter the Lord CHIEF JUSTICE and his men

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: What is the matter? Keep the peace here, ho!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Good my lord, be good to me. I beseech you stand to me.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
How now, Sir John? What, are you brawling here? Doth
this become your place, your time, and business? You
should have been well on your way to York. — [to FANG]
Stand from him, fellow: wherefore hang’st thou upon
him?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
O my most worshipful lord, an ’t please your Grace, I
am a poor widow of Eastcheap, and he is arrested at my
suit.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: For what sum?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
It is more than for some, my lord; it is for all I
have. He hath eaten me out of house and home. He hath
put all my substance into that fat belly of his. [to
FALSTAFF] But I will have some of it out again, or I
will ride thee o' nights like the mare.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I think I am as like to ride the mare if I have any
vantage of ground to get up.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
How comes this, Sir John? Fie, what man of good temper
would endure this tempest of exclamation? Are you not
ashamed to enforce a poor widow to so rough a course to
come by her own?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
[to MISTRESS QUICKLY] What is the gross sum that I owe
thee?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Marry, if thou wert an honest man, thyself and the
money too. Thou didst swear to me upon a parcel-gilt
goblet, sitting in my Dolphin chamber at the round table
by a sea-coal fire, upon Wednesday in Wheeson week,
when the Prince broke thy head for liking his father to
a singing-man of Windsor, thou didst swear to me then,
as I was washing thy wound, to marry me and make me my
lady thy wife. Canst thou deny it? Did not goodwife
Keech, the butcher’s wife, come in then and call me
Gossip Quickly, coming in to borrow a mess of vinegar;
telling us she had a good dish of prawns, whereby thou
didst desire to eat some, whereby I told thee they were
ill for a green wound? And didst thou not, when she was
gone downstairs, desire me to be no more so familiarity
with such poor people, saying that ere long they should
call me madam? And didst thou not kiss me and bid me
fetch thee thirty shillings? I put thee now to thy
book-oath. Deny it if thou canst.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
My lord, this is a poor mad soul, and she says up and
down the town that her eldest son is like you. She hath
been in good case, and the truth is, poverty hath
distracted her. But, for these foolish officers, I
beseech you I may have redress against them.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Sir John, Sir John, I am well acquainted with your
manner of wrenching the true cause the false way. It is
not a confident brow, nor the throng of words that come
with such more than impudent sauciness from you, can
thrust me from a level consideration. You have, as it
appears to me, practiced upon the easy-yielding spirit
of this woman, and made her serve your uses both in
purse and in person.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Yea, in truth, my lord.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Pray thee, peace. — [to FALSTAFF] Pay her the debt you
owe her, and unpay the villany you have done her. The
one you may do with sterling money, and the other with
current repentance.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
My lord, I will not undergo this sneap without reply.
You call honorable boldness “impudent sauciness.” If a
man will make curtsy and say nothing, he is virtuous.
No, my lord, my humble duty remembered, I will not be
your suitor. I say to you, I do desire deliverance from
these officers, being upon hasty employment in the
King’s affairs.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
You speak as having power to do wrong; but answer in
th' effect of your reputation, and satisfy this poor
woman.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Come hither, hostess.
Enter GOWER

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Now, Master Gower, what news?

units.speaker: GOWER
units.text:
The King, my lord, and Harry Prince of Wales Are near
at hand. The rest the paper tells.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: As I am a gentleman!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Faith, you said so before.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: As I am a gentleman. Come. No more words of it.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
By this heavenly ground I tread on, I must be fain to
pawn both my plate and the tapestry of my dining
chambers.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Glasses, glasses, is the only drinking. And for thy
walls, a pretty slight drollery, or the story of the
Prodigal or the German hunting in waterwork is worth a
thousand of these bed-hangers and these fly-bitten
tapestries. Let it be ten pound, if thou canst. Come, an
’twere not for thy humors, there’s not a better wench
in England. Go wash thy face, and draw the action. Come,
thou must not be in this humor with me. Dost not know
me? Come, come, I know thou wast set on to this.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Pray thee, Sir John, let it be but twenty nobles. I'
faith, I am loath to pawn my plate, so God save me, la.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Let it alone. I’ll make other shift. You’ll be a fool
still.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Well, you shall have it, though I pawn my gown. I hope
you’ll come to supper. You’ll pay me all together?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Will I live? [to BARDOLPH] Go with her, with her. Hook
on, hook on.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Will you have Doll Tearsheet meet you at supper?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: No more words. Let’s have her.
Exeunt MISTRESS QUICKLY, FANG, SNARE, BARDOLPH, and the PAGE

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: I have heard better news.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: What’s the news, my good lord?

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Where lay the King last night?

units.speaker: GOWER
units.text: At Basingstoke, my lord.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: I hope, my lord, all’s well. What is the news, my lord?

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Come all his forces back?

units.speaker: GOWER
units.text:
No; fifteen hundred foot, five hundred horse Are
marched up to my Lord of Lancaster Against
Northumberland and the Archbishop.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Comes the King back from Wales, my noble lord?

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
You shall have letters of me presently. Come. Go along
with me, good Master Gower.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: My lord!

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Master Gower, shall I entreat you with me to dinner?

units.speaker: GOWER
units.text:
I must wait upon my good lord here. I thank you, good
Sir John.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Sir John, you loiter here too long, being you are to
take soldiers up in counties as you go.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Will you sup with me, Master Gower?

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: What foolish master taught you these manners, Sir John?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Master Gower, if they become me not, he was a fool that
taught them me.—This is the right fencing grace, my
lord: tap for tap, and so part fair.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: Now the Lord lighten thee. Thou art a great fool.
Exeunt
Enter PRINCE HENRY and POINS

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Before God, I am exceeding weary.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Is ’t come to that? I had thought weariness durst not
have attached one of so high blood.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Faith, it does me; though it discolors the complexion
of my greatness to acknowledge it. Doth it not show
vilely in me to desire small beer?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Why, a prince should not be so loosely studied as to
remember so weak a composition.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Belike then my appetite was not princely got, for, by
my troth, I do now remember the poor creature small
beer. But indeed these humble considerations make me out
of love with my greatness. What a disgrace is it to me
to remember thy name, or to know thy face tomorrow, or
to take note how many pair of silk stockings thou
hast—with these, and those that were thy peach-colored
ones —or to bear the inventory of thy shirts, as, one for
superfluity and another for use. But that the
tennis-court keeper knows better than I, for it is a low
ebb of linen with thee when thou keepest not racket
there, as thou hast not done a great while, because the
rest of the low countries have made a shift to eat up
thy holland; and God knows whether those that bawl out
the ruins of thy linen shall inherit His kingdom; but
the midwives say the children are not in the fault,
whereupon the world increases and kindreds are mightily
strengthened.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
How ill it follows, after you have labored so hard, you
should talk so idly! Tell me, how many good young
princes would do so, their fathers being so sick as
yours at this time is?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Shall I tell thee one thing, Poins?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Yes, faith, and let it be an excellent good thing.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
It shall serve among wits of no higher breeding than
thine.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Go to. I stand the push of your one thing that you will
tell.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Marry, I tell thee it is not meet that I should be sad,
now my father is sick —albeit I could tell thee, as to
one it pleases me, for fault of a better, to call my
friend, I could be sad, and sad indeed too.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Very hardly, upon such a subject.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
By this hand, thou thinkest me as far in the devil’s
book as thou and Falstaff for obduracy and persistency.
Let the end try the man. But I tell thee, my heart
bleeds inwardly that my father is so sick: and keeping
such vile company as thou art hath in reason taken from
me all ostentation of sorrow.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: The reason?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What wouldst thou think of me if I should weep?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: I would think thee a most princely hypocrite.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
It would be every man’s thought, and thou art a blessed
fellow to think as every man thinks. Never a man’s
thought in the world keeps the roadway better than
thine. Every man would think me an hypocrite indeed. And
what accites your most worshipful thought to think so?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Why, because you have been so lewd and so much
engraffed to Falstaff.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: And to thee.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
By this light, I am well spoke on. I can hear it with
my own ears. The worst that they can say of me is that I
am a second brother, and that I am a proper fellow of
my hands; and those two things, I confess, I cannot
help. By the Mass, here comes Bardolph.
Enter BARDOLPH and the PAGE

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
And the boy that I gave Falstaff. He had him from me
Christian, and look if the fat villain have not
transformed him ape.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: God save your Grace.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: And yours, most noble Bardolph.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
[to BARDOLPH] Come, you virtuous ass, you bashful fool,
must you be blushing? Wherefore blush you now? What a
maidenly man-at-arms are you become! Is ’t such a matter
to get a pottle-pot’s maidenhead?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
He calls me e'en now, my lord, through a red lattice,
and I could discern no part of his face from the window.
At last I spied his eyes, and methought he had made two
holes in the ale-wife’s new petticoat and so peeped
through.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Has not the boy profited?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Away, you whoreson upright rabbit, away!

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Away, you rascally Althea’s dream, away!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Instruct us, boy. What dream, boy?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Marry, my lord, Althea dreamt she was delivered of a
firebrand, and therefore I call him her dream.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
A crown’s worth of good interpretation. There ’tis,
boy.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
O, that this good blossom could be kept from cankers!
Well, there is sixpence to preserve thee.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
An you do not make him hanged among you, the gallows
shall have wrong.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: And how doth thy master, Bardolph?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
Well, my good lord. He heard of your Grace’s coming to
town. There’s a letter for you.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Delivered with good respect. And how doth the Martlemas
your master?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: In bodily health, sir.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Marry, the immortal part needs a physician, but that
moves not him. Though that be sick, it dies not.

[reads to himself] I do allow this wen to be as
familiar with me as my dog, and he holds his place, for
look you how be writes. [he hands the letter to POINS]

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
[reads] John Falstaff, knight. Every man must know that
as oft as he has occasion to name himself, even like
those that are kin to the King , for they never prick
their finger but they say, “There’s some of the King’s
blood spilt.” “How comes that?” says he that takes upon
him not to conceive. The answer is as ready as a
borrower’s cap: “I am the King’s poor cousin, sir.”

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Nay, they will be kin to us, or they will fetch it from
Japheth. But to the letter. [takes the letter and
reads]Sir John alstaff, knight, to the son of the King
nearest his father, Harry Prince of Wales, greeting.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Why, this is a certificate.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Peace! [reads] I will imitate the honorable Romans in
brevity.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: He sure means brevity in breath, short-winded.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
[reads] I commend me to thee, I commend thee, and I
leave thee. Be not too familiar with Poins, for he
misuses thy favors so much that he swears thou art to
marry his sister Nell. Repent at idle times as thou
mayest, and so, farewell. Thine by yea and no, which is
as much as to say, as thou usest him, Jack Falstaff with
my familiars, John with my brothers and sisters, and
Sir John with all Europe.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
My lord, I’ll steep this letter in sack and make him
eat it.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
That’s to make him eat twenty of his words. But do you
use me thus, Ned? Must I marry your sister?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
God send the wench no worse fortune! But I never said
so.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Well, thus we play the fools with the time, and the
spirits of the wise sit in the clouds and mock us. [to
BARDOLPH] Is your master here in London?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Yea, my lord.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Where sups he? Doth the old boar feed in the old frank?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: At the old place, my lord, in Eastcheap.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What company?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Ephesians, my lord, of the old church.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Sup any women with him?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
None, my lord, but old Mistress Quickly and Mistress
Doll Tearsheet.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What pagan may that be?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
A proper gentlewoman, sir, and a kinswoman of my
master’s.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Even such kin as the parish heifers are to the town
bull.— Shall we steal upon them, Ned, at supper?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: I am your shadow, my lord. I’ll follow you.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Sirrah—you, boy—and Bardolph, no word to your master
that I am yet come to town. [gives them money] There’s
for your silence.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: I have no tongue, sir.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: And for mine, sir, I will govern it.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Fare you well. Go.
Exeunt BARDOLPH and PAGE

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: This Doll Tearsheet should be some road.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
I warrant you, as common as the way between Saint
Alban’s and London.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
How might we see Falstaff bestow himself tonight in his
true colors, and not ourselves be seen?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Put on two leathern jerkins and aprons, and wait upon
him at his table as drawers.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
From a god to a bull: a heavy decension. It was Jove’s
case. From a prince to a 'prentice: a low transformation
that shall be mine, for in everything the purpose must
weigh with the folly. Follow me, Ned.
Exeunt
Enter NORTHUMBERLAND, LADY NORTHUMBERLAND, and LADY PERCY

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
I pray thee, loving wife and gentle daughter, Give even
way unto my rough affairs. Put not you on the visage of
the times And be, like them, to Percy troublesome.

units.speaker: LADY NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
I have given over. I will speak no more. Do what you
will; your wisdom be your guide.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Alas, sweet wife, my honor is at pawn, And, but my
going, nothing can redeem it.

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text:
O yet, for God’s sake, go not to these wars. The time
was, father, that you broke your word, When you were
more endeared to it than now, When your own Percy, when
my heart’s dear Harry, Threw many a northward look to
see his father Bring up his powers; but he did long in
vain. Who then persuaded you to stay at home? There were
two honors lost, yours and your son’s. For yours, the
God of heaven brighten it. For his, it stuck upon him as
the sun In the gray vault of heaven , and by his light
Did all the chivalry of England move To do brave acts. He
was indeed the glass Wherein the noble youth did dress
themselves. He had no legs that practiced not his gait;
And speaking thick, which nature made his blemish, Became
the accents of the valiant; For those that could speak
low and tardily Would turn their own perfection to abuse
To seem like him. So that in speech, in gait, In diet,
in affections of delight, In military rules, humors of
blood, He was the mark and glass, copy and book, That
fashioned others. And him—O wondrous him! O miracle of
men!—him did you leave, Second to none, unseconded by
you, To look upon the hideous god of war In
disadvantage, to abide a field Where nothing but the
sound of Hotspur’s name Did seem defensible. So you left
him . Never, O never, do his ghost the wrong To hold
your honor more precise and nice With others than with
him. Let them alone. The Marshal and the Archbishop are
strong. Had my sweet Harry had but half their numbers,
Today might I, hanging on Hotspur’s neck, Have talked of
Monmouth’s grave.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Beshrew your heart, Fair daughter, you do draw my
spirits from me With new lamenting ancient oversights.
But I must go and meet with danger there, Or it will seek
me in another place And find me worse provided.

units.speaker: LADY NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Oh, fly to Scotland Till that the nobles and the
armèd commons Have of their puissance made a little
taste.

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text:
If they get ground and vantage of the King, Then join
you with them like a rib of steel To make strength
stronger; but, for all our loves, First let them try
themselves. So did your son; He was so suffered. So came
I a wid ow, And never shall have length of life enough
To rain upon remembrance with mine eyes That it may grow
and sprout as high as heaven For recordation to my noble
husband.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Come, come, go in with me. 'Tis with my mind As with
the tide swelled up unto his height, That makes a
still-stand, running neither way. Fain would I go to
meet the Archbishop, But many thousand reasons hold me
back. I will resolve for Scotland. There am I Till time
and vantage crave my company.
Exeunt
Enter two DRAWERS

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text:
What the devil hast thou brought there—applejohns? Thou
knowest Sir John cannot endure an applejohn.

units.speaker: SECOND DRAWER
units.text:
Mass, thou sayest true. The Prince once set a dish of
applejohns before him and told him there were five more
Sir Johns and, putting off his hat, said “I will now
take my leave of these six dry, round, old, withered
knights.” It angered him to the heart. But he hath
forgot that.

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text:
Why then, cover, and set them down, and see if thou
canst find out Sneak’s noise. Mistress Tearsheet would
fain hear some music.
Enter THIRD DRAWER

units.speaker: THIRD DRAWER
units.text:
Dispatch: the room where they supped is too hot.
They’ll come in straight.

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text:
Sirrah, here will be the Prince and Master Poins anon,
and they will put on two of our jerkins and aprons, and
Sir John must not know of it. Bardolph hath brought
word.

units.speaker: THIRD DRAWER
units.text:
By the Mass, here will be old utis. It will be an
excellent stratagem.

units.speaker: SECOND DRAWER
units.text: I’ll see if I can find out Sneak.
FRANCIS and THE DRAWERS exit
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY and DOLL TEARSHEET

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
I' faith, sweetheart, methinks now you are in an
excellent good temperality. Your pulsidge beats as
extraordinarily as heart would desire, and your color, I
warrant you, is as red as any rose, in good truth, la .
But, i' faith, you have drunk too much canaries, and
that’s a marvellous searching wine, and it perfumes the
blood ere one can say “What’s this?” How do you now?

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Better than I was. Hem.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Why, that’s well said. A good heart’s worth gold. Lo,
here comes Sir John.
Enter FALSTAFF

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
[sings] When Arthur first in court —Empty the jordan.
[sings] And was a worthy king — How now, Mistress Doll?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Sick of a calm, yea, good faith.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
So is all her sect. An they be once in a calm, they are
sick.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
A pox damn you, muddy rascal. Is that all the comfort
you give me?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: You make fat rascals, Mistress Doll.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
I make them? Gluttony and diseases make them; I make
them not.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
If the cook help to make the gluttony, you help to make
the diseases, Doll. We catch of you, Doll, we catch of
you. Grant that, my poor virtue, grant that.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Yea, joy, our chains and our jewels.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Your broaches, pearls, and ouches—for to serve bravely
is to come halting off, you know; to come off the breach
with his pike bent bravely, and to surgery bravely, to
venture upon the charged chambers bravely—

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Hang yourself, you muddy conger, hang yourself!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
By my troth, this is the old fashion. You two never
meet but you fall to some discord. You are both, i' good
truth, as rheumatic as two dry toasts. You cannot one
bear with another’s confirmities. What the good-year!
One must bear, and that must be you. You are the weaker
vessel, as they say, the emptier vessel.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Can a weak empty vessel bear such a huge full hogshead?
There’s a whole merchant’s venture of Bourdeaux stuff
in him. You have not seen a hulk better stuffed in the
hold. — Come, I’ll be friends with thee, Jack. Thou art
going to the wars, and whether I shall ever see thee
again or no, there is nobody cares.
Enter FIRST DRAWER

units.speaker: FIRST DRAWER
units.text: Sir, Ancient Pistol’s below and would speak with you.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Hang him, swaggering rascal! Let him not come hither.
It is the foul-mouthed’st rogue in England.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
If he swagger, let him not come here. No, by my faith,
I must live among my neighbors. I’ll no swaggerers: I am
in good name and fame with the very best. S hut the
door. There comes no swaggerers here. I have not lived
all this while to have swaggering now. Shut the door, I
pray you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Dost thou hear, hostess?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Pray you pacify yourself, Sir John. There comes no
swaggerers here.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Dost thou hear? It is mine ancient.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Tilly-vally, Sir John, ne'er tell me. And your ancient
swaggerer comes not in my doors. I was before Master
Tisick, the debuty t' other day, and, as he said to
me—’twas no longer ago than Wednesday last, i' good
faith— “Neighbour Quickly,” says he—Master Dumb, our
minister, was by then—“Neighbour Quickly,” says he,
“receive those that are civil, for,” said he, “you are
in an ill name.” Now he said so, I can tell whereupon.
“For,” says he, “you are an honest woman, and well
thought on. Therefore take heed what guests you receive.
Receive,” says he, “no swaggering companions.” There
comes none here. You would bless you to hear what he
said. No, I’ll no swaggerers.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
He’s no swaggerer, hostess, a tame cheater, i' faith.
You may stroke him as gently as a puppy greyhound. He’ll
not swagger with a Barbary hen if her feathers turn
back in any show of resistance. —Call him up, drawer.
Exit FIRST DRAWER

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
“Cheater,” call you him? I will bar no honest man my
house, nor no cheater, but I do not love swaggering. By
my troth, I am the worse when one says “swagger.” Feel,
masters, how I shake; look you, I warrant you.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: So you do, hostess.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Do I? Yea, in very truth, do I, an ’twere an aspen
leaf. I cannot abide swaggerers.
Enter PISTOL, BARDOLPH, and the PAGE

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: God save you, Sir John.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Welcome, Ancient Pistol. Here, Pistol, I charge you
with a cup of sack. Do you discharge upon mine hostess.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: I will discharge upon her, Sir John, with two bullets.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
She is pistol-proof. Sir, you shall not hardly offend
her.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Come, I’ll drink no proofs nor no bullets. I’ll drink
no more than will do me good, for no man’s pleasure, I.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: Then to you, Mistress Dorothy! I will charge you.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Charge me! I scorn you, scurvy companion. What, you
poor, base, rascally, cheating lack-linen mate! Away,
you mouldy rogue, away! I am meat for your master.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: I know you, Mistress Dorothy.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Away, you cutpurse rascal, you filthy bung, away! By
this wine, I’ll thrust my knife in your mouldy chaps an
you play the saucy cuttle with me. Away, you bottle-ale
rascal, you basket-hilt stale juggler, you. Since when,
I pray you, sir? God’s light, with two points on your
shoulder? Much!

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
God let me not live, but I will murder your ruff for
this.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
No more, Pistol. I would not have you go off here.
Discharge yourself of our company, Pistol.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: No, good Captain Pistol, not here, sweet captain.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Captain? Thou abominable damned cheater, art thou not
ashamed to be called captain? An captains were of my
mind, they would truncheon you out for taking their
names upon you before you have earned them. You a
captain? You slave, for what? For tearing a poor whore’s
ruff in a bawdy house? He a captain! Hang him, rogue.
He lives upon mouldy stewed prunes and dried cakes. A
captain? God’s light, these villains will make the word
as odious as the word “occupy,” which was an excellent
good word before it was ill sorted. Therefore captains
had need look to ’t.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Pray thee go down, good ancient.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Hark thee hither, Mistress Doll.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Not I. I tell thee what, Corporal Bardolph, I could
tear her. I’ll be revenged of her.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Pray thee go down.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
I’ll see her damned first to Pluto’s damnèd lake, by
this hand, to th' infernal deep with Erebus and tortures
vile also. Hold hook and line, say I. Down, down, dogs!
Down, Fates! Have we not Hiren here?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Good Captain Peesell, be quiet. 'Tis very late, i'
faith. I beseek you now, aggravate your choler.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
These be good humors indeed. Shall pack-horses And
hollow pampered jades of Asia, which cannot go but
thirty mile a day, Compare with Caesars and with
cannibals, and Troyant Greeks? Nay, rather damn
them with King Cerberus, and let the welkin roar.
Shall we fall foul for toys?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: By my troth, captain, these are very bitter words.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Begone, good ancient. This will grow to a brawl anon.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Die men like dogs! Give crowns like pins! Have we not
Hiren here?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
O' my word, captain, there’s none such here. What the
good- year, do you think I would deny her? For God’s
sake, be quiet.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Then feed and be fat, my fair Calipolis. Come, give ’s
some sack. Si fortune me tormente, sperato me contento.
Fear we broadsides? No, let the fiend give fire. Give me
some sack, and, sweetheart, lie thou there. [lays down
his sword] Come we to full points here? And are
etceteras nothing?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Pistol, I would be quiet.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Sweet knight, I kiss thy neaf. What, we have seen the
seven stars.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
For God’s sake, thrust him downstairs. I cannot endure
such a fustian rascal.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: “Thrust him downstairs?" Know we not Galloway nags?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Quoit him down, Bardolph, like a shove-groat shilling.
Nay, an he do nothing but speak nothing, he shall be
nothing here.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Come, get you downstairs.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
What! shall we have incision? Shall we imbrue? [
snatches up his sword] Then death rock me asleep, abridge
my doleful days. Why then, let grievous, ghastly,
gaping wounds untwine the Sisters Three. Come, Atropos,
I say.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Here’s goodly stuff toward!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Give me my rapier, boy.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: I pray thee, Jack, I pray thee do not draw.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Get you downstairs. [drawing and driving PISTOL out]

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Here’s a goodly tumult. I’ll forswear keeping house
afore I’ll be in these tirrits and frights. So, murder,
I warrant now. Alas, alas, put up your naked weapons,
put up your naked weapons.
Exeunt PISTOL pursued by BARDOLPH

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
I pray thee, Jack, be quiet. The rascal’s gone. Ah, you
whoreson little valiant villain, you.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Are you not hurt i' the groin? Methought he made a
shrewd thrust at your belly.
Enter BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Have you turned him out o' doors?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
Yea, sir. The rascal’s drunk. You have hurt him, sir,
i' the shoulder.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: A rascal to brave me!

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Ah, you sweet little rogue, you. Alas, poor ape, how
thou sweat’st! Come, let me wipe thy face. Come on, you
whoreson chops. Ah, rogue, i' faith, I love thee. Thou
art as valorous as Hector of Troy, worth five of
Agamemnon, and ten times better than the Nine Worthies.
Ah, villain!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Ah, rascally slave! I will toss the rogue in a blanket.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Do, an thou darest for thy heart. An thou dost, I’ll
canvass thee between a pair of sheets.
Enter musicians

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: The music is come, sir.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Let them play.—Play, sirs.—Sit on my knee, Doll. A
rascal bragging slave! The rogue fled from me like
quicksilver.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
I' faith, and thou followed’st him like a church. Thou
whoreson little tidy Bartholomew boar-pig , when wilt thou
leave fighting a-days and foining a-nights and begin to
patch up thine old body for heaven?
Enter, behind, PRINCE HENRY and POINS, disguised as drawers

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Peace, good Doll. Do not speak like a death’s-head; do
not bid me remember mine end.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Sirrah, what humor’s the Prince of?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A good shallow young fellow, he would have made a good
pantler; he would a' chipped bread well.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: They say Poins has a good wit.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
He a good wit? Hang him, baboon. His wit’s as thick as
Tewksbury mustard. There’s no more conceit in him than
is in a mallet.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Why does the Prince love him so then?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Because their legs are both of a bigness, and he plays
at quoits well, and eats conger and fennel, and drinks
off candles' ends for flap-dragons, and rides the wild
mare with the boys, and jumps upon joint stools, and
swears with a good grace, and wears his boots very
smooth, like unto the sign of the Leg, and breeds no
bate with telling of discreet stories, and such other
gambol faculties he has that show a weak mind and an
able body, for the which the Prince admits him; for the
Prince himself is such another. The weight of a hair
will turn the scales between their avoirdupois.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
[To POINS] Would not this nave of a wheel have his ears
cut off?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Let’s beat him before his whore.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Look whe'er the withered elder hath not his poll clawed
like a parrot.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Is it not strange that desire should so many years
outlive performance?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Kiss me, Doll.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Saturn and Venus this year in conjunction! What says
th' almanac to that?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
And look whether the fiery trigon, his man, be not
lisping to his master’s old tables, his notebook, his
counsel keeper.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: [to DOLL] Thou dost give me flattering busses.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: By my troth, I kiss thee with a most constant heart.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: I am old, I am old.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
I love thee better than I love e'er a scurvy young boy
of them all.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What stuff wilt have a kirtle of? I shall receive money
o' Thursday; shalt have a cap tomorrow. A merry song!
Come, it grows late. We’ll to bed. Thou 'lt forget me
when I am gone.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
By my troth, thou 'lt set me a-weeping an thou sayest
so. Prove that ever I dress myself handsome till thy
return. Well, harken a' th' end.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Some sack, Francis.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY AND POINS
units.text: Anon, anon, sir.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Ha? A bastard son of the King’s?—And art not thou Poins
his brother?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Why, thou globe of sinful continents, what a life dost
thou lead?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A better than thou. I am a gentleman. Thou art a
drawer.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Very true, sir, and I come to draw you out by the ears.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
O, the Lord preserve thy good Grace! By my troth,
welcome to London. Now the Lord bless that sweet face of
thine. O Jesu, are you come from Wales?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Thou whoreson mad compound of majesty, [indicating
DOLL] by this light flesh and corrupt blood, thou art
welcome.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: How? You fat fool, I scorn you.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
My lord, he will drive you out of your revenge and turn
all to a merriment, if you take not the heat.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
You whoreson candle-mine, you how vilely did you speak
of me even now before this honest, virtuous, civil
gentlewoman!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
God’s blessing of your good heart, and so she is, by my
troth.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Didst thou hear me?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Yea, and you knew me, as you did when you ran away by
Gad’s Hill. You knew I was at your back, and spoke it on
purpose to try my patience.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
No, no, no; not so. I did not think thou wast within
hearing.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
I shall drive you, then, to confess the wilfull abuse,
and then I know how to handle you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: No abuse, Hal, o' mine honor, no abuse.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Not to dispraise me and call me pantier and
bread-chipper and I know not what?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: No abuse, Hal.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: No abuse?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
No abuse, Ned, i' th' world, honest Ned, none. I
dispraised him before the wicked, that the wicked might
not fall in love with thee; in which doing, I have done
the part of a careful friend and a true subject, and thy
father is to give me thanks for it. No abuse, Hal.
—None, Ned, none. No, faith, boys, none.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
See now whether pure fear and entire cowardice doth not
make thee wrong this virtuous gentlewoman to close with
us. Is she of the wicked, is thine hostess here of the
wicked, or is thy boy of the wicked, or honest Bardolph,
whose zeal burns in his nose, of the wicked?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Answer, thou dead elm, answer.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
The fiend hath pricked down Bardolph irrecoverable, and
his face is Lucifer’s privy kitchen, where he doth
nothing but roast malt-worms. For the boy, there is a
good angel about him, but the devil outbids him too.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: For the women?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
For one of them, she’s in hell already and burns poor
souls. For the other, I owe her money, and whether she
be damned for that I know not.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: No, I warrant you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
No, I think thou art not; I think thou art quit for
that. Marry, there is another indictment upon thee for
suffering flesh to be eaten in thy house contrary to the
law, for the which I think thou wilt howl.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
All vitlars do so. What’s a joint of mutton or two in a
whole Lent?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: You, gentlewoman.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: What says your Grace?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: His grace says that which his flesh rebels against.
Knocking within

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Who knocks so loud at door? Look to th' door there,
Francis.
Enter PETO

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Peto, how now, what news?

units.speaker: PETO
units.text:
The King your father is at Westminster, And there are
twenty weak and wearied posts Come from the north, and
as I came along I met and overtook a dozen captains,
Bareheaded, sweating, knocking at the taverns And asking
everyone for Sir John Falstaff.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
By heaven, Poins, I feel me much to blame So idly to
profane the precious time When tempest of commotion,
like the south Borne with black vapour, doth begin to
melt And drop upon our bare unarmèd heads. — Give me my
sword and cloak. —Falstaff, good night.
Exeunt PRINCE HENRY, POINS, PETO and BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Now comes in the sweetest morsel of the night, and we
must hence and leave it unpicked.
Knocking within

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: More knocking at the door?
Enter BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: How now, what’s the matter?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
You must away to court, sir, presently. A dozen
captains stay at door for you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
[to the PAGE] Pay the musicians, sirrah. —Farewell,
hostess .—Farewell, Doll. You see, my good wenches, how
men of merit are sought after. The undeserver may sleep
when the man of action is called on. Farewell, good
wenches. If I be not sent away post, I will see you again
ere I go.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
I cannot speak. If my heart be not ready to burst—well,
sweet Jack, have a care of thyself.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Farewell, farewell.
Exeunt FALSTAFF, BARDOLPH, PAGE, and musicians

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Well, fare thee well. I have known thee these
twenty-nine years, come peascod time, but an honester
and truer-hearted man—well, fare thee well.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: [within] Mistress Tearsheet!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: [within] Bid Mistress Tearsheet come to my master.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
O, run, Doll, run, run, good Doll. Come.—She comes
blubbered.—Yea! Will you come, Doll?
Exeunt
Enter KING Henry in his nightgown, with a page

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
Go call the Earls of Surrey and of Warwick; But, ere
they come, bid them o'erread these letters And well
consider of them. Make good speed.
Exit page

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
How many thousand of my poorest subjects Are at this
hour asleep! O sleep, O gentle sleep, Nature’s soft
nurse, how have I frighted thee, That thou no more wilt
weigh my eyelids down And steep my senses in
forgetfulness? Why rather, sleep, liest thou in smoky
cribs, Upon uneasy pallets stretching thee And hushed
with buzzing night-flies to thy slumber, Than in the
perfumed chambers of the great, Under the canopies of
costly state, And lull’d with sound of sweetest melody?
O thou dull god, why liest thou with the vile In
loathsome beds and leavest the kingly couch A watch-case
or a common 'larum bell? Wilt thou upon the high and
giddy mast Seal up the shipboy’s eyes, and rock his
brains In cradle of the rude imperious surge And in the
visitation of the winds, Who take the ruffian billows by
the top, Curling their monstrous heads and hanging them
With deafening clamor in the slippery clouds That with
the hurly death itself awakes? Canst thou, O partial
sleep, give thy repose To the wet sea-boy in an hour so
rude, And, in the calmest and most stillest night, With
all appliances and means to boot, Deny it to a king?
Then, happy low, lie down. Uneasy lies the head that
wears a crown.
Enter WARWICK and SURREY

units.speaker: WARWICK
units.text: Many good morrows to your Majesty.

units.speaker: KING
units.text: Is it good morrow, lords?

units.speaker: WARWICK
units.text: 'Tis one o'clock, and past.

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
