bear. The heart’s all.
Exit DAVY

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
Be merry, Master Bardolph. —And, my little soldier
there, be merry.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text:
[sings] Be merry, be merry, my wife has all, For women
are shrews, both short and tall. 'Tis merry in hall when
beards wag all, And welcome merry Shrovetide. Be merry,
be merry.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I did not think Master Silence had been a man of this
mettle.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text: Who, I? I have been merry twice and once ere now.
Enter DAVY

units.speaker: DAVY
units.text: [To BARDOLPH] There’s a dish of leather-coats for you.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: Davy!

units.speaker: DAVY
units.text:
Your Worship, I’ll be with you straight.— [to BARDOLPH]
A cup of wine, sir?

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text:
[sings] A cup of wine that’s brisk and fine, And drink
unto thee, leman mine, And a merry heart lives long-a.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Well said, Master Silence.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text:
And we shall be merry; now comes in the sweet o' th'
night.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Health and long life to you, Master Silence.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text:
[sings] Fill the cup, and let it come, I’ll pledge you
a mile to th' bottom.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
Honest Bardolph, welcome. If thou wantest anything and
wilt not call, beshrew thy heart. — [to the PAGE]
Welcome, my little tiny thief, and welcome indeed too.
I’ll drink to Master Bardolph, and to all the cabileros
about London.

units.speaker: DAVY
units.text: I hope to see London once ere I die.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: And I might see you there, Davy!

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
By the Mass, you’ll crack a quart together, ha, will
you not, Master Bardolph?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Yea, sir, in a pottle-pot.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
By God’s liggens, I thank thee. The knave will stick by
thee, I can assure thee that. He will not out, he. 'Tis
true bred!

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: And I’ll stick by him, sir.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
Why, there spoke a king. Lack nothing, be merry. [One
knocks at door.] Look who’s at door there, ho. Who
knocks?
Exit DAVY

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: [to SILENCE] Why, now you have done me right.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text:
[sings] Do me right, And dub me knight, Samingo. Is ’t
not so?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: 'Tis so.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text: Is ’t so? Why then, say an old man can do somewhat.
Enter DAVY

units.speaker: DAVY
units.text:
An ’t please your Worship, there’s one Pistol come from
the court with news.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: From the court? Let him come in.
Enter PISTOL

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: How now, Pistol?

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: Sir John, God save you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: What wind blew you hither, Pistol?

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Not the ill wind which blows no man to good. Sweet
knight, thou art now one of the greatest men in this
realm.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text: By 'r Lady, I think he be, but Goodman Puff of Barson.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Puff? Puff in thy teeth, most recreant coward base!—
Sir John, I am thy Pistol and thy friend, And
helter-skelter have I rode to thee, And tidings do I
bring, and lucky joys, And golden times, and happy news
of price.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: I pray thee now, deliver them like a man of this world.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
A foutre for the world and worldlings base! I speak of
Africa and golden joys.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
O base Assyrian knight, what is thy news? Let King
Cophetua know the truth thereof.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text: [sings] And Robin Hood, Scarlet, and John.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Shall dunghill curs confront the Helicons, And shall
good news be baffled? Then, Pistol, lay thy head in
Furies' lap.

units.speaker: SILENCE
units.text: Honest gentleman, I know not your breeding.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: Why then, lament therefor.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
Give me pardon, sir. If, sir, you come with news from
the court, I take it there’s but two ways, either to
utter them, or to conceal them. I am, sir, under the
King in some authority.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: Under which king, besonian? Speak or die.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: Under King Harry.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: Harry the Fourth, or Fifth?

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: Harry the Fourth.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
A foutre for thine office!— Sir John, thy tender
lambkin now is king. Harry the Fifth’s the man. I speak
the truth. When Pistol lies, do this [he makes an
obscene gesture] and fig me, like The bragging
Spaniard.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: What, is the old king dead?

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: As nail in door. The things I speak are just.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Away, Bardolph.—Saddle my horse.—Master Robert Shallow,
choose what office thou wilt in the land, ’tis thine.
Pistol, I will double-charge thee with dignities.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
O joyful day! I would not take a knighthood for my
fortune.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: What, I do bring good news!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Carry Master Silence to bed.—Master Shallow, my Lord
Shallow, be what thou wilt. I am Fortune’s steward. Get
on thy boots. We’ll ride all night.—O sweet
Pistol!—Away, Bardolph!
Exit BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Come, Pistol, utter more to me, and withal devise
something to do thyself good. Boot, boot, Master
Shallow. I know the young King is sick for me. Let us
take any man’s horses. The laws of England are at my
commandment. Blessed are they that have been my friends,
and woe to my Lord Chief Justice!

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Let vultures vile seize on his lungs also! “Where is
the life that late I led?” say they. Why, here it is.
Welcome these pleasant days.
Exeunt
Enter BEADLES, dragging in MISTRESS QUICKLY and DOLL TEARSHEET

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
No, thou arrant knave. I would to God that I might die,
that I might have thee hanged. Thou hast drawn my
shoulder out of joint.

units.speaker: FIRST BEADLE
units.text:
The Constables have delivered her over to me, and she
shall have whipping cheer enough, I warrant her. There
hath been a man or two lately killed about her.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
Nut-hook, nut-hook, you lie! Come on, I ’ll tell thee
what, thou damned tripe-visaged rascal: an the child I
now go with do miscarry, thou wert better thou hadst
struck thy mother, thou paper-faced villain.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
O the Lord, that Sir John were come! I would make this
a bloody day to somebody. But I pray God the fruit of
her womb might miscarry.

units.speaker: FIRST BEADLE
units.text:
If it do, you shall have a dozen of cushions again; you
have but eleven now. Come, I charge you both go with
me, for the man is dead that you and Pistol beat amongst
you.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text:
I’ll tell you what, you thin man in a censer, I will
have you as soundly swinged for this, you bluebottle
rogue, you filthy famished correctioner. If you be not
swinged, I’ll forswear half-kirtles.

units.speaker: FIRST BEADLE
units.text: Come, come, you she knight-errant, come.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
O God, that right should thus overcome might! Well, of
sufferance comes ease.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Come, you rogue, come, bring me to a justice.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Ay, come, you starved bloodhound.

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Goodman Death, Goodman Bones!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Thou atomy, thou!

units.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
units.text: Come, you thin thing, come, you rascal.

units.speaker: FIRST BEADLE
units.text: Very well.
Exeunt
Enter two GROOMS, strewing rushes

units.speaker: FIRST GROOM
units.text: More rushes, more rushes.

units.speaker: SECOND GROOM
units.text: The trumpets have sounded twice.

units.speaker: FIRST GROOM
units.text:
'Twill be two o'clock ere they come from the
coronation. Dispatch, dispatch.
Exeunt
Enter FALSTAFF, SHALLOW, PISTOL, BARDOLPH, and PAGE

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Stand here by me, Master Robert Shallow. I will make
the King do you grace. I will leer upon him as he comes
by, and do but mark the countenance that he will give
me.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: God bless thy lungs, good knight!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Come here, Pistol, stand behind me. — [to SHALLOW ] O, if
I had had time to have made new liveries, I would have
bestowed the thousand pound I borrowed of you. But ’tis
no matter. This poor show doth better. This doth infer
the zeal I had to see him.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: It doth so.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: It shows my earnestness of affection—

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: It doth so.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: My devotion—

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: It doth, it doth, it doth.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
As it were, to ride day and night, and not to
deliberate, not to remember, not to have patience to
shift me—

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: It is best, certain.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
But to stand stained with travel and sweating with
desire to see him, thinking of nothing else, putting all
affairs else in oblivion, as if there were nothing else
to be done but to see him.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
'Tis semper idem , for obsque hoc nihil est ; ’tis all in
every part.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: 'Tis so indeed.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
My knight, I will inflame thy noble liver, and make
thee rage. Thy Doll and Helen of thy noble thoughts is
in base durance and contagious prison, Haled thither by
most mechanical and dirty hand. Rouse up revenge from
ebon den with fell Alecto’s snake, for Doll is in.
Pistol speaks nought but truth.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: I will deliver her.
Shouts within, and the trumpets sound

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: There roared the sea, and trumpet-clangor sounds.
Enter PRINCE HENRY and his train, the Lord CHIEF JUSTICE among them

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: God save thy Grace, King Hal, my royal Hal.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
The heavens thee guard and keep, most royal imp of
fame!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: God save thee, my sweet boy!

units.speaker: KING
units.text: My Lord Chief Justice, speak to that vain man.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
[to FALSTAFF] Have you your wits? Know you what ’tis to
speak?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: My King, my Jove, I speak to thee, my heart!

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
I know thee not, old man. Fall to thy prayers. How ill
white hairs become a fool and jester. I have long dreamt
of such a kind of man, So surfeit-swelled, so old, and
so profane; But being awaked, I do despise my dream.
Make less thy body hence, and more thy grace; Leave
gormandizing. Know the grave doth gape For thee thrice
wider than for other men. Reply not to me with a
fool-born jest. Presume not that I am the thing I was,
For God doth know—so shall the world perceive— That I
have turned away my former self. So will I those that
kept me company. When thou dost hear I am as I have
been, Approach me, and thou shalt be as thou wast, The
tutor and the feeder of my riots. Till then I banish
thee, on pain of death, As I have done the rest of my
misleaders, Not to come near our person by ten mile. For
competence of life I will allow you, That lack of means
enforce you not to evils. And, as we hear you do reform
yourselves, We will, according to your strengths and
qualities, Give you advancement. [to CHIEF JUSTICE ] Be
it your charge, my lord, To see performed the tenor of
my word.— [To the attendants] Set on.
Exeunt PRINCE HENRY, the CHIEF JUSTICE, and the attendants.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Master Shallow, I owe you a thousand pound.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
Yea, marry, Sir John, which I beseech you to let me
have home with me.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
That can hardly be, Master Shallow. Do not you grieve
at this. I shall be sent for in private to him. Look
you, he must seem thus to the world. Fear not your
advancements. I will be the man yet that shall make you
great.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
I cannot well perceive how, unless you should give me
your doublet and stuff me out with straw. I beseech you,
good Sir John, let me have five hundred of my thousand.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Sir, I will be as good as my word. This that you heard
was but a color.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: A color that I fear you will die in, Sir John.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Fear no colors. Go with me to dinner.—Come, Lieutenant
Pistol.—Come, Bardolph.—I shall be sent for soon at
night.
Enter the Lord CHIEF JUSTICE and Prince John of LANCASTER; officers with them

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
Go, carry Sir John Falstaff to the Fleet. Take all his
company along with him.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: My lord, my lord—

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text:
I cannot now speak. I will hear you soon.— Take them
away.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: Si fortune me tormenta, spero me contenta.
Exeunt all but Prince John of LANCASTER andthe CHIEF JUSTICE

units.speaker: LANCASTER
units.text:
I like this fair proceeding of the King’s. He hath
intent his wonted followers Shall all be very well
provided for, But all are banished till their
conversations Appear more wise and modest to the world.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: And so they are.

units.speaker: LANCASTER
units.text: The King hath called his parliament, my lord.

units.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
units.text: He hath.

units.speaker: LANCASTER
units.text:
I will lay odds that, ere this year expire, We bear our
civil swords and native fire As far as France: I beard
a bird so sing, Whose music, to my thinking, pleased the
King. Come, will you hence?
Exeunt
Enter the EPILOGUE.

units.speaker: EPILOGUE
units.text:
First my fear; then my curtsy, last my speech. My fear
is your displeasure my curtsy my duty; and my speech, to
beg your pardons. If you look for a good speech now, you
undo me, for what I have to say is of mine own making,
and what indeed I should say will, I doubt, prove mine
own marring. But to the purpose, and so to the venture.
Be it known to you, as it is very well, I was lately here
in the end of a displeasing play to pray your patience
for it and to promise you a better. I meant indeed to pay
you with this, which, if like an ill venture it come
unluckily home, I break, and you, my gentle creditors,
lose. Here I promised you I would be, and here I commit
my body to your mercies. Bate me some, and I will pay you
some, and, as most debtors do, promise you infinitely.
And so I kneel down before you, but, indeed, to pray for
the Queen. If my tongue cannot entreat you to acquit me,
will you command me to use my legs? And yet that were but
light payment, to dance out of your debt. But a good
conscience will make any possible satisfaction, and so
would I. All the gentlewomen here have forgiven me; if
the gentlemen will not, then the gentlemen do not agree
with the gentlewomen, which was never seen before in such
an assembly. One word more, I beseech you: if you be not
too much cloyed with fat meat, our humble author will
continue the story, with Sir John in it, and make you
merry with fair Katherine of France , where, for anything
I know, Falstaff shall die of a sweat, unless already he
be killed with your hard opinions; for Oldcastle died a
martyr, and this is not the man. My tongue is weary; when
my legs are too, I will bid you good night.
Exits

sentences.text: Enter RUMOR all painted with tongues

sentences.speaker: RUMOR
sentences.text:
Open your ears, for which of you will stop The vent of
hearing when loud Rumor speaks? I, from the orient to
the drooping west, Making the wind my post-horse, still
unfold The acts commenced on this ball of earth. Upon my
tongues continual slanders ride, The which in every
language I pronounce, Stuffing the ears of men with
false reports. I speak of peace while covert enmity
Under the smile of safety wounds the world. And who but
Rumor, who but only I, Make fearful musters and prepared
defense, Whiles the big year, swoll'n with some other
grief, Is thought with child by the stern tyrant war,
And no such matter? Rumor is a pipe Blown by surmises,
jealousies, conjectures, And of so easy and so plain a
stop That the blunt monster with uncounted heads, The
still-discordant wav'ring multitude, Can play upon it.
But what need I thus My well-known body to anatomize
Among my household? Why is Rumor here? I run before King
Harry’s victory, Who in a bloody field by Shrewsbury
Hath beaten down young Hotspur and his troops, Quenching
the flame of bold rebellion Even with the rebels' blood.
But what mean I To speak so true at first? My office is
To noise abroad that Harry Monmouth fell Under the
wrath of noble Hotspur’s sword, And that the King before
the Douglas' rage Stooped his anointed head as low as
death. This have I rumored through the peasant towns
Between that royal field of Shrewsbury And this
worm-eaten hold of ragged stone, Where Hotspur’s father,
old Northumberland, Lies crafty-sick. The posts come
tiring on, And not a man of them brings other news Than
they have learnt of me. From Rumor’s tongues They bring
smooth comforts false, worse than true wrongs.

still-discordant wav'ring multitude, Can play upon it.
But what need I thus My well-known body to anatomize
Among my household? Why is Rumor here? I run before King
Harry’s victory, Who in a bloody field by Shrewsbury
Hath beaten down young Hotspur and his troops, Quenching
the flame of bold rebellion Even with the rebels' blood.
But what mean I To speak so true at first? My office is
To noise abroad that Harry Monmouth fell Under the
wrath of noble Hotspur’s sword, And that the King before
the Douglas' rage Stooped his anointed head as low as
death. This have I rumored through the peasant towns
Between that royal field of Shrewsbury And this
worm-eaten hold of ragged stone, Where Hotspur’s father,
old Northumberland, Lies crafty-sick. The posts come
tiring on, And not a man of them brings other news Than
they have learnt of me. From Rumor’s tongues They bring
smooth comforts false, worse than true wrongs.

sentences.speaker: RUMOR
sentences.text:
Open your ears, for which of you will stop The vent of
hearing when loud Rumor speaks? I, from the orient to
the drooping west, Making the wind my post-horse, still
unfold The acts commenced on this ball of earth. Upon my
tongues continual slanders ride, The which in every
language I pronounce, Stuffing the ears of men with
false reports. I speak of peace while covert enmity
Under the smile of safety wounds the world. And who but
Rumor, who but only I, Make fearful musters and prepared
defense, Whiles the big year, swoll'n with some other
grief, Is thought with child by the stern tyrant war,
And no such matter? Rumor is a pipe Blown by surmises,
jealousies, conjectures, And of so easy and so plain a
stop That the blunt monster with uncounted heads, The
still-discordant wav'ring multitude, Can play upon it.

Enter LORD BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Who keeps the gate here, ho?
Enter the PORTER

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Where is the Earl?

sentences.speaker: PORTER
sentences.text: What shall I say you are?

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Tell thou the Earl That the Lord Bardolph doth
attend him here.

sentences.speaker: PORTER
sentences.text:
His lordship is walked forth into the orchard. Please
it your Honor knock but at the gate And he himself will
answer.

sentences.speaker: PORTER
sentences.text:
His lordship is walked forth into the orchard. Please
it your Honor knock but at the gate And he himself will
answer.
NORTHUMBERLAND Enter

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Here comes the Earl.
Exit PORTER

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
What news, Lord Bardolph? Every minute now Should be
the father of some stratagem. The times are wild.
Contention, like a horse Full of high feeding, madly hath
broke loose And bears down all before him.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
What news, Lord Bardolph? Every minute now Should be
the father of some stratagem. The times are wild.
Contention, like a horse Full of high feeding, madly hath
broke loose And bears down all before him.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
What news, Lord Bardolph? Every minute now Should be
the father of some stratagem. The times are wild.
Contention, like a horse Full of high feeding, madly hath
broke loose And bears down all before him.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
What news, Lord Bardolph? Every minute now Should be
the father of some stratagem. The times are wild.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Noble Earl, I bring you certain news from
Shrewsbury.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text: Good, an God will!

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
As good as heart can wish. The King is almost wounded
to the death, And, in the fortune of my lord your son,
Prince Harry slain outright; and both the Blunts Killed
by the hand of Douglas; young Prince John And
Westmoreland and Stafford fled the field; And Harry
Monmouth’s brawn, the hulk Sir John, Is prisoner to your
son. O, such a day, So fought, so followed, and so
fairly won, Came not till now to dignify the times Since
Caesar’s fortunes.

How is this derived? Saw you the field? Came you
from Shrewsbury?

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
How is this derived? Saw you the field? Came you
from Shrewsbury?

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
How is this derived? Saw you the field? Came you
from Shrewsbury?

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
I spake with one, my lord, that came from thence, A
gentleman well bred and of good name, That freely
rendered me these news for true.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
I spake with one, my lord, that came from thence, A
gentleman well bred and of good name, That freely
rendered me these news for true.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Here comes my servant Travers, who I sent On Tuesday
last to listen after news.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Here comes my servant Travers, who I sent On Tuesday
last to listen after news.
Enter TRAVERS

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
My lord, I overrode him on the way; And he is furnished
with no certainties More than he haply may retail from
me.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
My lord, I overrode him on the way; And he is furnished
with no certainties More than he haply may retail from
me.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text: Now, Travers, what good tidings comes with you?

sentences.speaker: TRAVERS
sentences.text:
My lord, Sir John Umfrevile turned me back With joyful
tidings and, being better horsed, Outrode me. After him
came spurring hard A gentleman, almost forspent with
speed, That stopp’d by me to breathe his bloodied horse.
He asked the way to Chester, and of him I did demand
what news from Shrewsbury. He told me that rebellion had
bad luck And that young Harry Percy’s spur was cold.
With that he gave his able horse the head And, bending
forward, struck his armèd heels Against the panting
sides of his poor jade Up to the rowel-head, and
starting so He seemed in running to devour the way,
Staying no longer question.

sentences.text:
Ha? Again: Said he young Harry Percy’s spur was
cold? Of Hotspur, Coldspur? That rebellion Had met ill
luck?

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
My lord, I’ll tell you what: If my young lord your
son have not the day, Upon mine honor, for a silken
point I’ll give my barony. Never talk of it.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Why should that gentleman that rode by Travers Give
then such instances of loss?

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Who, he? He was some hilding fellow that had stolen
The horse he rode on and, upon my life, Spoke at a
venture. Look, here comes more news.

Enter MORTON

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Yea, this man’s brow, like to a title leaf, Foretells
the nature of a tragic volume. So looks the strond
whereon the imperious flood Hath left a witness’d
usurpation. — Say, Morton, didst thou come from
Shrewsbury?

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Yea, this man’s brow, like to a title leaf, Foretells
the nature of a tragic volume. So looks the strond
whereon the imperious flood Hath left a witness’d
usurpation. — Say, Morton, didst thou come from
Shrewsbury?

sentences.speaker: MORTON
sentences.text:
I ran from Shrewsbury, my noble lord, Where hateful
death put on his ugliest mask To fright our party.

sentences.speaker: MORTON
sentences.text:
I ran from Shrewsbury, my noble lord, Where hateful
death put on his ugliest mask To fright our party.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
How doth my son and brother? Thou tremblest, and the
whiteness in thy cheek Is apter than thy tongue to tell
thy errand. Even such a man, so faint, so spiritless, So
dull, so dead in look, so woebegone, Drew Priam’s
curtain in the dead of night, And would have told him
half his Troy was burnt; But Priam found the fire ere he
his tongue, And I my Percy’s death ere thou report’st
it. This thou wouldst say, “Your son did thus and thus;
Your brother thus; so fought the noble Douglas”— Stopping
my greedy ear with their bold deeds. But in the end, to
stop my ear indeed, Thou hast a sigh to blow away this
praise, Ending with “Brother, son, and all are dead.”

sentences.text:
Douglas is living, and your brother yet, But for my
lord your son—

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Why, he is dead. See what a ready tongue suspicion
hath! He that but fears the thing he would not know
Hath, by instinct, knowledge from others' eyes That what
he feared is chancèd . Yet speak, Morton. Tell thou an
earl his divination lies, And I will take it as a sweet
disgrace And make thee rich for doing me such wrong.

sentences.speaker: MORTON
sentences.text:
You are too great to be by me gainsaid, Your spirit is
too true, your fears too certain.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Yet, for all this, say not that Percy’s dead. I see a
strange confession in thine eye. Thou shak’st thy head
and hold’st it fear or sin To speak a truth. If he be
slain, say so. The tongue offends not that reports his
death; And he doth sin that doth belie the dead, Not he
which says the dead is not alive. Yet the first bringer
of unwelcome news Hath but a losing office, and his
tongue Sounds ever after as a sullen bell Remembered
tolling a departing friend.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: I cannot think, my lord, your son is dead.

sentences.speaker: MORTON
sentences.text:
I am sorry I should force you to believe That which I
would to God I had not seen, But these mine eyes saw him
in bloody state, Rend'ring faint quittance, wearied and
outbreathed, To Harry Monmouth; whose swift wrath beat
down The never-daunted Percy to the earth, From whence
with life he never more sprung up. In few, his death,
whose spirit lent a fire Even to the dullest peasant in
his camp, Being bruited once, took fire and heat away
From the best tempered courage in his troops; For from
his metal was his party steeled, Which, once in him
abated, all the rest Turned on themselves, like dull and
heavy lead. And as the thing that’s heavy in itself
Upon enforcement flies with greatest speed, So did our
men, heavy in Hotspur’s loss, Lend to this weight such
lightness with their fear That arrows fled not swifter
toward their aim Than did our soldiers, aiming at their
safety, Fly from the field. Then was the noble Worcester
Too soon ta'en prisoner; and that furious Scot, The
bloody Douglas, whose well-laboring sword Had three
times slain th'appearance of the King, Gan vail his
stomach and did grace the shame Of those that turned
their backs and in his flight, Stumbling in fear, was
took. The sum of all Is that the King hath won and hath
sent out A speedy power to encounter you, my lord, Under
the conduct of young Lancaster And Westmoreland. This
is the news at full.

From the best tempered courage in his troops; For from
his metal was his party steeled, Which, once in him
abated, all the rest Turned on themselves, like dull and
heavy lead. And as the thing that’s heavy in itself
Upon enforcement flies with greatest speed, So did our
men, heavy in Hotspur’s loss, Lend to this weight such
lightness with their fear That arrows fled not swifter
toward their aim Than did our soldiers, aiming at their
safety, Fly from the field. Then was the noble Worcester
Too soon ta'en prisoner; and that furious Scot, The
bloody Douglas, whose well-laboring sword Had three
times slain th'appearance of the King, Gan vail his
stomach and did grace the shame Of those that turned
their backs and in his flight, Stumbling in fear, was
took. The sum of all Is that the King hath won and hath
sent out A speedy power to encounter you, my lord, Under
the conduct of young Lancaster And Westmoreland. This
is the news at full.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
For this I shall have time enough to mourn. In poison
there is physic, and these news, Having been well, that
would have made me sick, Being sick, have in some
measure made me well. And as the wretch whose
fever-weakened joints, Like strengthless hinges, buckle
under life, Impatient of his fit, breaks like a fire Out
of his keeper’s arms, even so my limbs, Weakened with
grief, being now enraged with grief, Are thrice
themselves. Hence therefore, thou nice crutch. A scaly
gauntlet now with joints of steel Must glove this hand.
And hence, thou sickly coif. Thou art a guard too wanton
for the head Which princes, fleshed with conquest, aim
to hit. Now bind my brows with iron, and approach The
ragged’st hour that time and spite dare bring To frown
upon th'enraged Northumberland. Let heaven kiss earth!
Now let not Nature’s hand Keep the wild flood confined.
Let order die, And let this world no longer be a stage
To feed contention in a lingering act; But let one spirit
of the firstborn Cain Reign in all bosoms, that, each
heart being set On bloody courses, the rude scene may
end, And darkness be the burier of the dead.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: This strainèd passion doth you wrong, my lord.

sentences.speaker: MORTON
sentences.text:
Sweet Earl, divorce not wisdom from your honor. The
lives of all your loving complices Lean on your health,
the which, if you give o'er To stormy passion, must
perforce decay. You cast th' event of war, my noble
lord, And summed the account of chance before you said
“Let us make head.” It was your presurmise That, in the
dole of blows your son might drop. You knew he walked
o'er perils on an edge, More likely to fall in than to
get o'er. You were advised his flesh was capable Of
wounds and scars, and that his forward spirit Would lift
him where most trade of danger ranged. Yet did you say
“Go forth,” and none of this, Though strongly
apprehended, could restrain The stiff-borne action. What
hath then befall'n, Or what did this bold enterprise
brought forth, More than that being which was like to
be?

apprehended, could restrain The stiff-borne action. What
hath then befall'n, Or what did this bold enterprise
brought forth, More than that being which was like to
be?

sentences.speaker: MORTON
sentences.text:
Sweet Earl, divorce not wisdom from your honor. The
lives of all your loving complices Lean on your health,
the which, if you give o'er To stormy passion, must
perforce decay. You cast th' event of war, my noble
lord, And summed the account of chance before you said
“Let us make head.” It was your presurmise That, in the
dole of blows your son might drop. You knew he walked
o'er perils on an edge, More likely to fall in than to
get o'er. You were advised his flesh was capable Of
wounds and scars, and that his forward spirit Would lift
him where most trade of danger ranged. Yet did you say
“Go forth,” and none of this, Though strongly
apprehended, could restrain The stiff-borne action. What
hath then befall'n, Or what did this bold enterprise
brought forth, More than that being which was like to
be?

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
We all that are engagèd to this loss Knew that we
ventured on such dangerous seas That if we wrought out
life, ’twas ten to one; And yet we ventured, for the
gain proposed Choked the respect of likely peril feared;
And since we are o'erset, venture again. Come, we will
all put forth, body and goods.

sentences.speaker: MORTON
sentences.text:
'Tis more than time.—And, my most noble lord, I hear
for certain, and do speak the truth: The gentle
Archbishop of York is up With well-appointed powers. He
is a man Who with a double surety binds his followers.
My lord your son had only but the corpse, But shadows and
the shows of men, to fight; For that same word
“rebellion” did divide The action of their bodies from
their souls, And they did fight with queasiness,
constrained, As men drink potions, that their weapons
only Seemed on our side. But, for their spirits and
souls, This word “rebellion,” it had froze them up As
fish are in a pond. But now the Bishop Turns
insurrection to religion. Supposed sincere and holy in
his thoughts, He’s followed both with body and with
mind, And doth enlarge his rising with the blood Of fair
King Richard, scraped from Pomfret stones; Derives from
heaven his quarrel and his cause; Tells them he doth
bestride a bleeding land, Gasping for life under great
Bolingbroke; And more and less do flock to follow him.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
I knew of this before, but, to speak truth, This
present grief had wiped it from my mind. Go in with me
and counsel every man The aptest way for safety and
revenge. Get posts and letters, and make friends with
speed. Never so few, and never yet more need.

Exeunt
Enter Sir John FALSTAFF, with his PAGE bearing his sword and buckler

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Sirrah, you giant, what says the doctor to my water?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
He said, sir, the water itself was a good healthy
water, but, for the party that owed it, he might have
more diseases than he knew for.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Men of all sorts take a pride to gird at me. The brain
of this foolish-compounded clay, man, is not able to
invent anything that tends to laughter more than I
invent, or is invented on me. I am not only witty in
myself, but the cause that wit is in other men. I do
here walk before thee like a sow that hath overwhelmed
all her litter but one. If the Prince put thee into my
service for any other reason than to set me off, why
then I have no judgment. Thou whoreson mandrake, thou
art fitter to be worn in my cap than to wait at my
heels. I was never manned with an agate till now, but I
will inset you neither in gold nor silver, but in vile
apparel, and send you back again to your master for a
jewel. The juvenal, the Prince your master, whose chin
is not yet fledge—I will sooner have a beard grow in the
palm of my hand than he shall get one off his cheek,
and yet he will not stick to say his face is a face
royal. God may finish it when He will. 'Tis not a hair
amiss yet. He may keep it still at a face royal, for a
barber shall never earn sixpence out of it, and yet
he’ll be crowing as if he had writ man ever since his
father was a bachelor. He may keep his own grace, but
he’s almost out of mine, I can assure him. What said
Master Dommelton about the satin for my short cloak and
my slops?

apparel, and send you back again to your master for a
jewel. The juvenal, the Prince your master, whose chin
is not yet fledge—I will sooner have a beard grow in the
palm of my hand than he shall get one off his cheek,
and yet he will not stick to say his face is a face
royal. God may finish it when He will. 'Tis not a hair
amiss yet. He may keep it still at a face royal, for a
barber shall never earn sixpence out of it, and yet
he’ll be crowing as if he had writ man ever since his
father was a bachelor. He may keep his own grace, but
he’s almost out of mine, I can assure him. What said
Master Dommelton about the satin for my short cloak and
my slops?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
He said, sir, you should procure him better assurance
than Bardolph. He would not take his band and yours. He
liked not the security.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
He said, sir, you should procure him better assurance
than Bardolph. He would not take his band and yours. He
liked not the security.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
He said, sir, you should procure him better assurance
than Bardolph. He would not take his band and yours. He
liked not the security.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Let him be damned like the glutton! Pray God his tongue
be hotter! A whoreson Achitophel, a rascally
yea-forsooth knave, to bear a gentleman in hand and then
stand upon security! The whoreson smoothy-pates do now
wear nothing but high shoes and bunches of keys at their
girdles; and if a man is through with them in honest
taking up, then they must stand upon security. I had as
lief they would put ratsbane in my mouth as offer to
stop it with “security.” I looked he should have sent me
two-and-twenty yards of satin, as I am a true knight,
and he sends me “security.” Well, he may sleep in
security, for he hath the horn of abundance, and the
lightness of his wife shines through it, and yet cannot
he see though he have his own lantern to light him.
Where’s Bardolph?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: He’s gone into Smithfield to buy your Worship a horse.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I bought him in Paul’s, and he’ll buy me a horse in
Smithfield. An I could get me but a wife in the stews, I
were manned, horsed, and wived.

Enter the Lord CHIEF JUSTICE and SERVANT

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
Sir, here comes the nobleman that committed the Prince
for striking him about Bardolph.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Wait close. I will not see him.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What’s he that goes there?

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text: Falstaff, an ’t please your Lordship.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: He that was in question for the robbery?

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text:
He, my lord; but he hath since done good service at
Shrewsbury, and, as I hear, is now going with some
charge to the Lord John of Lancaster.

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text:
He, my lord; but he hath since done good service at
Shrewsbury, and, as I hear, is now going with some
charge to the Lord John of Lancaster.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What, to York? Call him back again.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What, to York? Call him back again.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What, to York? Call him back again.

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text: Sir John Falstaff!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Boy, tell him I am deaf.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: You must speak louder. My master is deaf.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: You must speak louder. My master is deaf.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
I am sure he is, to the hearing of any thing good.—Go
pluck him by the elbow. I must speak with him.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
I am sure he is, to the hearing of any thing good.—Go
pluck him by the elbow. I must speak with him.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
I am sure he is, to the hearing of any thing good.—Go
pluck him by the elbow. I must speak with him.

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text: Sir John!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What, a young knave and begging? Is there not wars? Is
there not employment? Doth not the King lack subjects? Do
not the rebels need soldiers? Though it be a shame to
be on any side but one, it is worse shame to beg than to
be on the worst side, were it worse than the name of
rebellion can tell how to make it.

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text: You mistake me, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Why sir, did I say you were an honest man? Setting my
knighthood and my soldiership aside, I had lied in my
throat if I had said so.

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text:
I pray you, sir, then set your knighthood and our
soldiership aside, and give me leave to tell you, you
lie in your throat if you say I am any other than an
honest man.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I give thee leave to tell me so? I lay aside that which
grows to me? If thou gett’st any leave of me, hang me;
if thou tak’st leave, thou wert better be hanged. You
hunt counter. Hence! Avaunt!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

hunt counter. Hence! Avaunt!

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text: Sir, my lord would speak with you.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Sir John Falstaff, a word with you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
My good lord. God give your Lordship good time of the
day. I am glad to see your Lordship abroad. I heard say
your Lordship was sick: I hope your Lordship goes abroad
by advice. Your Lordship, though not clean past your
youth, have yet some smack of an age in you, some relish
of the saltness of time in you, and I most humbly
beseech your Lordship to have a reverent care of your
health.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Sir John, I sent for you before your expedition to
Shrewsbury.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
An ’t please your Lordship, I hear his Majesty is
returned with some discomfort from Wales.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
I talk not of his Majesty. You would not come when I
sent for you.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
I talk not of his Majesty. You would not come when I
sent for you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
And I hear, moreover, his Highness is fallen into this
same whoreson apoplexy.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Well, God mend him. I pray you let me speak with you.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Well, God mend him. I pray you let me speak with you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
This apoplexy, as I take it, is a kind of lethargy , an
’t please your Lordship, a kind of sleeping in the
blood, a whoreson tingling.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
This apoplexy, as I take it, is a kind of lethargy , an
’t please your Lordship, a kind of sleeping in the
blood, a whoreson tingling.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What tell you me of it? Be it as it is.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What tell you me of it? Be it as it is.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
It hath its original from much grief, from study, and
perturbation of the brain. I have read the cause of his
effects in Galen. It is a kind of deafness.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
It hath its original from much grief, from study, and
perturbation of the brain. I have read the cause of his
effects in Galen. It is a kind of deafness.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
It hath its original from much grief, from study, and
perturbation of the brain. I have read the cause of his
effects in Galen. It is a kind of deafness.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
I think you are fallen into the disease, for you hear
not what I say to you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Very well, my lord, very well. Rather, an ’t please
you, it is the disease of not listening, the malady of
not marking, that I am troubled withal.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Very well, my lord, very well. Rather, an ’t please
you, it is the disease of not listening, the malady of
not marking, that I am troubled withal.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
To punish you by the heels would amend the attention of
your ears, and I care not if I do become your
physician.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I am as poor as Job, my lord, but not so patient. Your
Lordship may minister the potion of imprisonment to me in
respect of poverty, but how should I be your patient to
follow your prescriptions, the wise may make some dram
of a scruple, or indeed a scruple itself.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
I sent for you, when there were matters against you for
your life, to come speak with me.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
As I was then advised by my learned counsel in the laws
of this land-service, I did not come.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Well, the truth is, Sir John, you live in great infamy.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: He that buckles him in my belt cannot live in less.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Your means are very slender, and your waste is great.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would it were otherwise. I would my means were
greater and my waist slender.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would it were otherwise. I would my means were
greater and my waist slender.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: You have misled the youthful Prince.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
The young Prince hath misled me. I am the fellow with
the great belly, and he my dog.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
The young Prince hath misled me. I am the fellow with
the great belly, and he my dog.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Well, I am loath to gall a new-healed wound. Your day’s
service at Shrewsbury hath a little gilded over your
night’s exploit on Gad’s Hill. You may thank th' unquiet
time for your quiet o'erposting that action.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:

service at Shrewsbury hath a little gilded over your
night’s exploit on Gad’s Hill. You may thank th' unquiet
time for your quiet o'erposting that action.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: My lord!

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
But since all is well, keep it so. Wake not a sleeping
wolf.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
But since all is well, keep it so. Wake not a sleeping
wolf.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: To wake a wolf is as bad as to smell a fox.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What, you are as a candle, the better part burnt out.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What, you are as a candle, the better part burnt out.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A wassail candle, my lord, all tallow. If I did say of
wax, my growth would approve the truth.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A wassail candle, my lord, all tallow. If I did say of
wax, my growth would approve the truth.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
There is not a white hair on your face but should have
his effect of gravity.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: His effect of gravy, gravy, gravy.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
You follow the young Prince up and down like his ill
angel.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Not so, my lord. Your ill angel is light, but I hope he
that looks upon me will take me without weighing. And
yet in some respects I grant I cannot go. I cannot tell.
Virtue is of so little regard in these costermongers'
times that true valor is turned bear-herd; pregnancy is
made a tapster, and hath his quick wit wasted in giving
reckonings. All the other gifts appurtenant to man, as
the malice of this age shapes them, are not worth a
gooseberry. You that are old consider not the capacities
of us that are young. You do measure the heat of our
livers with the bitterness of your galls , and we that
are in the vaward of our youth, I must confess, are wags
too.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Do you set down your name in the scroll of youth, that
are written down old with all the characters of age?
Have you not a moist eye, a dry hand, a yellow cheek, a
white beard, a decreasing leg, an increasing belly? Is
not your voice broken, your wind short, your chin
double, your wit single, and every part about you
blasted with antiquity? And will you yet call yourself
young? Fie, fie, fie, Sir John

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
My lord, I was born about three of the clock in the
afternoon, with a white head and something a round
belly. For my voice, I have lost it with halloing and
singing of anthems. To approve my youth further, I will
not. The truth is, I am only old in judgment and
understanding. And he that will caper with me for a
thousand marks, let him lend me the money, and have at
him! For the box of the ear that the Prince gave you, he
gave it like a rude prince, and you took it like a
sensible lord. I have checked him for it, and the young
lion repents. Marry, not in ashes and sackcloth, but in
new silk and old sack.

sentences.text:
My lord, I was born about three of the clock in the
afternoon, with a white head and something a round
belly. For my voice, I have lost it with halloing and
singing of anthems. To approve my youth further, I will
not. The truth is, I am only old in judgment and
understanding. And he that will caper with me for a
thousand marks, let him lend me the money, and have at
him! For the box of the ear that the Prince gave you, he
gave it like a rude prince, and you took it like a
sensible lord. I have checked him for it, and the young
lion repents. Marry, not in ashes and sackcloth, but in
new silk and old sack.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Well, God send the Prince a better companion.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
God send the companion a better prince. I cannot rid my
hands of him.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
God send the companion a better prince. I cannot rid my
hands of him.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Well, the King hath severed you and Prince Harry. I
hear you are going with Lord John of Lancaster against
the Archbishop and the Earl of Northumberland.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Well, the King hath severed you and Prince Harry. I
hear you are going with Lord John of Lancaster against
the Archbishop and the Earl of Northumberland.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Yea, I thank your pretty sweet wit for it. But look you
pray, all you that kiss my Lady Peace at home, that our
armies join not in a hot day, for, by the Lord, I take
but two shirts out with me, and I mean not to sweat
extraordinarily. If it be a hot day and I brandish
anything but a bottle, I would I might never spit white
again. There is not a dangerous action can peep out his
head but I am thrust upon it. Well, I cannot last ever.
But it was always yet the trick of our English nation,
if they have a good thing, to make it too common. If ye
will needs say I am an old man, you should give me rest.
I would to God my name were not so terrible to the
enemy as it is. I were better to be eaten to death with
a rust than to be scoured to nothing with perpetual
motion.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Well, be honest, be honest; and God bless your
expedition!

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Well, be honest, be honest; and God bless your
expedition!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Will your Lordship lend me a thousand pound to furnish
me forth?

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Not a penny, not a penny. You are too impatient to bear
crosses. Fare you well. Commend me to my cousin
Westmoreland.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
If I do, fillip me with a three-man beetle. A man can
no more separate age and covetousness than he can part
young limbs and lechery; but the gout galls the one, and
the pox pinches the other, and so both the degrees
prevent my curses. —Boy!

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: Sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What money is in my purse?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: Seven groats and two pence.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I can get no remedy against this consumption of the
purse. Borrowing only lingers and lingers it out, but
the disease is incurable. Go bear this letter to my Lord
of Lancaster, this to the Prince, this to the Earl of
Westmoreland; and this to old Mistress Ursula, whom I
have weekly sworn to marry since I perceived the first
white hair on my chin . About it. You know where to find
me.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A pox of this gout! Or, a gout of this pox, for the one
or the other plays the rogue with my great toe. 'Tis no
matter if I do halt. I have the wars for my color, and
my pension shall seem the more reasonable. A good wit
will make use of anything. I will turn diseases to
commodity.

Enter the ARCHBISHOP of York, Thomas MOWBRAY the Earl Marshal, Lord HASTINGS, and LORD BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Thus have you heard our cause and known our means, And,
my most noble friends, I pray you all Speak plainly
your opinions of our hopes. And first, Lord Marshal,
what say you to it?

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Thus have you heard our cause and known our means, And,
my most noble friends, I pray you all Speak plainly
your opinions of our hopes. And first, Lord Marshal,
what say you to it?

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Thus have you heard our cause and known our means, And,
my most noble friends, I pray you all Speak plainly
your opinions of our hopes. And first, Lord Marshal,
what say you to it?

sentences.speaker: MOWBRAY
sentences.text:
I well allow the occasion of our arms, But gladly would
be better satisfied How in our means we should advance
ourselves To look with forehead bold and big enough Upon
the power and puissance of the King.

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:
Our present musters grow upon the file To
five-and-twenty thousand men of choice, And our supplies
live largely in the hope Of great Northumberland, whose
bosom burns With an incensèd fire of injuries.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
The question then, Lord Hastings, standeth thus:
Whether our present five-and-twenty thousand May hold up
head without Northumberland.

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text: With him we may.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Yea, marry, there’s the point. But if without him we
be thought too feeble, My judgment is we should not
step too far Till we had his assistance by the hand. For
in a theme so bloody-faced as this, Conjecture,
expectation, and surmise Of aids incertain should not be
admitted.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Yea, marry, there’s the point. But if without him we
be thought too feeble, My judgment is we should not
step too far Till we had his assistance by the hand. For

admitted.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
'Tis very true, Lord Bardolph; for indeed It was young
Hotspur’s cause at Shrewsbury.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
'Tis very true, Lord Bardolph; for indeed It was young
Hotspur’s cause at Shrewsbury.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
It was, my lord; who lined himself with hope, Eating
the air on promise of supply, Flatt'ring himself in
project of a power Much smaller than the smallest of his
thoughts, And so, with great imagination Proper to
madmen, led his powers to death And, winking, leapt into
destruction.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:
But, by your leave, it never yet did hurt To lay down
likelihoods and forms of hope.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Yes, if this present quality of war— Indeed the instant
action, a cause on foot— Lives so in hope, as in an
early spring We see the appearing buds, which to prove
fruit Hope gives not so much warrant as despair That
frosts will bite them. When we mean to build, We first
survey the plot, then draw the model, And when we see
the figure of the house, Then must we rate the cost of
the erection, Which if we find outweighs ability, What
do we then but draw anew the model In fewer offices, or
at last desist To build at all? Much more in this great
work, Which is almost to pluck a kingdom down And set
another up, should we survey The plot of situation and
the model, Consent upon a sure foundation, Question
surveyors, know our own estate, How able such a work to
undergo, To weigh against his opposite. Or else We
fortify in paper and in figures, Using the names of men
instead of men, Like one that draws the model of a house
Beyond his power to build it , who, half through, Gives
o'er and leaves his part-created cost A naked subject to
the weeping clouds And waste for churlish winter’s
tyranny.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Yes, if this present quality of war— Indeed the instant
action, a cause on foot— Lives so in hope, as in an
early spring We see the appearing buds, which to prove
fruit Hope gives not so much warrant as despair That
frosts will bite them. When we mean to build, We first
survey the plot, then draw the model, And when we see
the figure of the house, Then must we rate the cost of
the erection, Which if we find outweighs ability, What
do we then but draw anew the model In fewer offices, or
at last desist To build at all? Much more in this great
work, Which is almost to pluck a kingdom down And set
another up, should we survey The plot of situation and
the model, Consent upon a sure foundation, Question
surveyors, know our own estate, How able such a work to
undergo, To weigh against his opposite. Or else We
fortify in paper and in figures, Using the names of men
instead of men, Like one that draws the model of a house
Beyond his power to build it , who, half through, Gives
o'er and leaves his part-created cost A naked subject to
the weeping clouds And waste for churlish winter’s
tyranny.

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:
Grant that our hopes, yet likely of fair birth, Should
be stillborn and that we now possessed The utmost man of
expectation, I think we are a body strong enough, Even
as we are, to equal with the King.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: What, is the King but five-and twenty-thousand?

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: What, is the King but five-and twenty-thousand?

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:
To us no more, nay, not so much, Lord Bardolph, For his
divisions, as the times do brawl, Are in three heads:
one power against the French, And one against Glendower;
perforce a third Must take up us. So is the unfirm King
In three divided, and his coffers sound With hollow
poverty and emptiness.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
That he should draw his several strengths together And
come against us in full puissance Need not be dreaded.

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:
If he should do so, He leaves his back unarmed, the
French and Welsh Baying him at the heels. Never fear
that.

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:
If he should do so, He leaves his back unarmed, the
French and Welsh Baying him at the heels. Never fear
that.

sentences.speaker: LORD BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Who is it like should lead his forces hither?

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:
The Duke of Lancaster and Westmoreland; Against the
Welsh, himself and Harry Monmouth; But who is
substituted against the French I have no certain notice.

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text:

substituted against the French I have no certain notice.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Let us on, And publish the occasion of our arms. The
commonwealth is sick of their own choice. Their
over-greedy love hath surfeited. An habitation giddy and
unsure Hath he that buildeth on the vulgar heart. O
thou fond many, with what loud applause Didst thou beat
heaven with blessing Bolingbroke Before he was what thou
wouldst have him be. And being now trimmed in thine own
desires, Thou, beastly feeder, art so full of him That
thou provok’st thyself to cast him up. So, so, thou
common dog, didst thou disgorge Thy glutton bosom of the
royal Richard, And now thou wouldst eat thy dead vomit
up And howl’st to find it. What trust is in these times?
They that, when Richard lived, would have him die Are
now become enamored on his grave. Thou, that threw’st
dust upon his goodly head When through proud London he
came sighing on After th' admired heels of Bolingbroke,
Criest now “O earth, yield us that King again, And take
thou this!” O thoughts of men accursed! Past and to come
seems best; things present, worst.

They that, when Richard lived, would have him die Are
now become enamored on his grave. Thou, that threw’st
dust upon his goodly head When through proud London he
came sighing on After th' admired heels of Bolingbroke,
Criest now “O earth, yield us that King again, And take
thou this!” O thoughts of men accursed! Past and to come
seems best; things present, worst.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Let us on, And publish the occasion of our arms. The
commonwealth is sick of their own choice. Their
over-greedy love hath surfeited. An habitation giddy and
unsure Hath he that buildeth on the vulgar heart. O
thou fond many, with what loud applause Didst thou beat
heaven with blessing Bolingbroke Before he was what thou
wouldst have him be. And being now trimmed in thine own
desires, Thou, beastly feeder, art so full of him That
thou provok’st thyself to cast him up. So, so, thou
common dog, didst thou disgorge Thy glutton bosom of the
royal Richard, And now thou wouldst eat thy dead vomit
up And howl’st to find it. What trust is in these times?
They that, when Richard lived, would have him die Are
now become enamored on his grave. Thou, that threw’st
dust upon his goodly head When through proud London he
came sighing on After th' admired heels of Bolingbroke,
Criest now “O earth, yield us that King again, And take
thou this!” O thoughts of men accursed! Past and to come
seems best; things present, worst.

sentences.speaker: MOWBRAY
sentences.text: Shall we go draw our numbers and set on?

sentences.speaker: HASTINGS
sentences.text: We are time’s subjects, and time bids begone.
Exeunt
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY, with two officers; FANG with her and SNARE following

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Master Fang, have you entered the action?

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: It is entered.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Where’s your yeoman? Is ’t a lusty yeoman? Will a'
stand to ’t?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Where’s your yeoman? Is ’t a lusty yeoman? Will a'
stand to ’t?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Where’s your yeoman? Is ’t a lusty yeoman? Will a'
stand to ’t?

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: Sirrah! Where’s Snare?

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: Sirrah! Where’s Snare?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: O Lord, ay, good Master Snare.

sentences.speaker: SNARE
sentences.text: Here, here.

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: Snare, we must arrest Sir John Falstaff.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Yea, good Master Snare, I have entered him and all.

sentences.speaker: SNARE
sentences.text:
It may chance cost some of us our lives, for he will
stab.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Alas the day, take heed of him. He stabbed me in mine
own house, and that most beastly, in good faith. He
cares not what mischief he does. If his weapon be out,
he will foin like any devil. He will spare neither man,
woman, nor child.

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: If I can close with him, I care not for his thrust.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: No, nor I neither. I’ll be at your elbow.

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: An I but fist him once, an he come but within my vue—

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
I am undone by his going. I warrant you, he’s an
infinitive thing upon my score. Good Master Fang, hold
him sure. Good Master Snare, let him not ’scape. He
comes continuantly to Pie Corner, saving your manhoods,
to buy a saddle, and he is indited to dinner to the
Lubber’s Head in Lumbert Street, to Master Smooth’s the
silkman. I pray you, since my exion is entered, and my
case so openly known to the world, let him be brought in
to his answer. A hundred mark is a long one for a poor
lone woman to bear, and I have borne, and borne, and
borne, and have been fubbed off, and fubbed off, and
fubbed off from this day to that day, that it is a shame
to be thought on. There is no honesty in such dealing,
unless a woman should be made an ass and a beast to bear
every knave’s wrong. Yonder he comes, and that errant
malmsey-nose knave, Bardolph, with him. Do your offices,
do your offices, Master Fang and Master Snare, do me,
do me, do me your offices.

Enter FALSTAFF, BARDOLPH, and PAGE

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: How now! Whose mare’s dead? What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: How now! Whose mare’s dead? What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: How now! Whose mare’s dead? What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: Sir John, I arrest you at the suit of Mistress Quickly.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Away, varlets!—Draw, Bardolph. Cut me off the villain’s
head. Throw the quean in the channel.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Away, varlets!—Draw, Bardolph. Cut me off the villain’s
head. Throw the quean in the channel.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Away, varlets!—Draw, Bardolph. Cut me off the villain’s
head. Throw the quean in the channel.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Throw me in the channel? I’ll throw thee in the
channel. Wilt thou, wilt thou, thou bastardly rogue?
—Murder, murder! — Ah, thou honeysuckle villain, wilt thou
kill God’s officers and the King’s? Ah, thou honeyseed
rogue, thou art a honeyseed, a man-queller, and a
woman-queller.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Keep them off, Bardolph.

sentences.speaker: FANG
sentences.text: A rescue, a rescue!

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Good people, bring a rescue or two. — [to FALSTAFF ] Thou
wot, wot thou? Thou wot, wot ta? Do, do, thou rogue.
Do, thou hempseed.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Away, you scullion, you rampallion, you fustilarian!
I’ll tickle your catastrophe.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF

Enter the Lord CHIEF JUSTICE and his men

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What is the matter? Keep the peace here, ho!

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What is the matter? Keep the peace here, ho!

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Good my lord, be good to me. I beseech you stand to me.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Good my lord, be good to me. I beseech you stand to me.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
How now, Sir John? What, are you brawling here? Doth
this become your place, your time, and business? You
should have been well on your way to York. — [to FANG]
Stand from him, fellow: wherefore hang’st thou upon
him?

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
How now, Sir John? What, are you brawling here? Doth
this become your place, your time, and business? You
should have been well on your way to York. — [to FANG]
Stand from him, fellow: wherefore hang’st thou upon
him?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
O my most worshipful lord, an ’t please your Grace, I
am a poor widow of Eastcheap, and he is arrested at my
suit.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: For what sum?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
It is more than for some, my lord; it is for all I
have. He hath eaten me out of house and home. He hath
put all my substance into that fat belly of his. [to
FALSTAFF] But I will have some of it out again, or I
will ride thee o' nights like the mare.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:

have. He hath eaten me out of house and home. He hath

will ride thee o' nights like the mare.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I think I am as like to ride the mare if I have any
vantage of ground to get up.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
How comes this, Sir John? Fie, what man of good temper
would endure this tempest of exclamation? Are you not
ashamed to enforce a poor widow to so rough a course to
come by her own?

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
[to MISTRESS QUICKLY] What is the gross sum that I owe
thee?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Marry, if thou wert an honest man, thyself and the
money too. Thou didst swear to me upon a parcel-gilt
goblet, sitting in my Dolphin chamber at the round table
by a sea-coal fire, upon Wednesday in Wheeson week,
when the Prince broke thy head for liking his father to
a singing-man of Windsor, thou didst swear to me then,
as I was washing thy wound, to marry me and make me my
lady thy wife. Canst thou deny it? Did not goodwife
Keech, the butcher’s wife, come in then and call me
Gossip Quickly, coming in to borrow a mess of vinegar;
telling us she had a good dish of prawns, whereby thou
didst desire to eat some, whereby I told thee they were
ill for a green wound? And didst thou not, when she was
gone downstairs, desire me to be no more so familiarity
with such poor people, saying that ere long they should
call me madam? And didst thou not kiss me and bid me
fetch thee thirty shillings? I put thee now to thy
book-oath. Deny it if thou canst.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
My lord, this is a poor mad soul, and she says up and
down the town that her eldest son is like you. She hath
been in good case, and the truth is, poverty hath
distracted her. But, for these foolish officers, I
beseech you I may have redress against them.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Sir John, Sir John, I am well acquainted with your
manner of wrenching the true cause the false way. It is
not a confident brow, nor the throng of words that come
with such more than impudent sauciness from you, can
thrust me from a level consideration. You have, as it
appears to me, practiced upon the easy-yielding spirit
of this woman, and made her serve your uses both in
purse and in person.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Yea, in truth, my lord.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Pray thee, peace. — [to FALSTAFF] Pay her the debt you
owe her, and unpay the villany you have done her. The
one you may do with sterling money, and the other with
current repentance.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Pray thee, peace. — [to FALSTAFF] Pay her the debt you
owe her, and unpay the villany you have done her. The
one you may do with sterling money, and the other with
current repentance.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
My lord, I will not undergo this sneap without reply.
You call honorable boldness “impudent sauciness.” If a
man will make curtsy and say nothing, he is virtuous.
No, my lord, my humble duty remembered, I will not be
your suitor. I say to you, I do desire deliverance from
these officers, being upon hasty employment in the
King’s affairs.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
You speak as having power to do wrong; but answer in
th' effect of your reputation, and satisfy this poor
woman.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
You speak as having power to do wrong; but answer in
th' effect of your reputation, and satisfy this poor
woman.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Come hither, hostess.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Come hither, hostess.
Enter GOWER

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Now, Master Gower, what news?

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
The King, my lord, and Harry Prince of Wales Are near
at hand. The rest the paper tells.

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
The King, my lord, and Harry Prince of Wales Are near
at hand. The rest the paper tells.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: As I am a gentleman!

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Faith, you said so before.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Faith, you said so before.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: As I am a gentleman. Come. No more words of it.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: As I am a gentleman. Come. No more words of it.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
By this heavenly ground I tread on, I must be fain to
pawn both my plate and the tapestry of my dining
chambers.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Glasses, glasses, is the only drinking. And for thy
walls, a pretty slight drollery, or the story of the
Prodigal or the German hunting in waterwork is worth a
thousand of these bed-hangers and these fly-bitten
tapestries. Let it be ten pound, if thou canst. Come, an
’twere not for thy humors, there’s not a better wench
in England. Go wash thy face, and draw the action. Come,
thou must not be in this humor with me. Dost not know
me? Come, come, I know thou wast set on to this.
