sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Pray thee, Sir John, let it be but twenty nobles. I'
faith, I am loath to pawn my plate, so God save me, la.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Pray thee, Sir John, let it be but twenty nobles. I'
faith, I am loath to pawn my plate, so God save me, la.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Let it alone. I’ll make other shift. You’ll be a fool
still.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Let it alone. I’ll make other shift. You’ll be a fool
still.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Let it alone. I’ll make other shift. You’ll be a fool
still.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Well, you shall have it, though I pawn my gown. I hope
you’ll come to supper. You’ll pay me all together?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Well, you shall have it, though I pawn my gown. I hope
you’ll come to supper. You’ll pay me all together?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Well, you shall have it, though I pawn my gown. I hope
you’ll come to supper. You’ll pay me all together?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Will I live? [to BARDOLPH] Go with her, with her. Hook
on, hook on.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Will I live? [to BARDOLPH] Go with her, with her. Hook
on, hook on.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Will you have Doll Tearsheet meet you at supper?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: No more words. Let’s have her.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: No more words. Let’s have her.
Exeunt MISTRESS QUICKLY, FANG, SNARE, BARDOLPH, and the PAGE

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: I have heard better news.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What’s the news, my good lord?

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Where lay the King last night?

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text: At Basingstoke, my lord.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: I hope, my lord, all’s well. What is the news, my lord?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: I hope, my lord, all’s well. What is the news, my lord?

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Come all his forces back?

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
No; fifteen hundred foot, five hundred horse Are
marched up to my Lord of Lancaster Against
Northumberland and the Archbishop.

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
No; fifteen hundred foot, five hundred horse Are
marched up to my Lord of Lancaster Against
Northumberland and the Archbishop.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Comes the King back from Wales, my noble lord?

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
You shall have letters of me presently. Come. Go along
with me, good Master Gower.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
You shall have letters of me presently. Come. Go along
with me, good Master Gower.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: My lord!

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Master Gower, shall I entreat you with me to dinner?

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
I must wait upon my good lord here. I thank you, good
Sir John.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Sir John, you loiter here too long, being you are to
take soldiers up in counties as you go.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text:
Sir John, you loiter here too long, being you are to
take soldiers up in counties as you go.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Will you sup with me, Master Gower?

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: What foolish master taught you these manners, Sir John?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Master Gower, if they become me not, he was a fool that
taught them me.—This is the right fencing grace, my
lord: tap for tap, and so part fair.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Now the Lord lighten thee. Thou art a great fool.

sentences.speaker: CHIEF JUSTICE
sentences.text: Now the Lord lighten thee. Thou art a great fool.
Exeunt
Enter PRINCE HENRY and POINS

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Before God, I am exceeding weary.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Is ’t come to that? I had thought weariness durst not
have attached one of so high blood.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Is ’t come to that? I had thought weariness durst not
have attached one of so high blood.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Faith, it does me; though it discolors the complexion
of my greatness to acknowledge it. Doth it not show
vilely in me to desire small beer?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Faith, it does me; though it discolors the complexion
of my greatness to acknowledge it. Doth it not show
vilely in me to desire small beer?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Faith, it does me; though it discolors the complexion
of my greatness to acknowledge it. Doth it not show
vilely in me to desire small beer?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Why, a prince should not be so loosely studied as to
remember so weak a composition.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Belike then my appetite was not princely got, for, by
my troth, I do now remember the poor creature small
beer. But indeed these humble considerations make me out
of love with my greatness. What a disgrace is it to me
to remember thy name, or to know thy face tomorrow, or
to take note how many pair of silk stockings thou
hast—with these, and those that were thy peach-colored
ones —or to bear the inventory of thy shirts, as, one for
superfluity and another for use. But that the
tennis-court keeper knows better than I, for it is a low
ebb of linen with thee when thou keepest not racket
there, as thou hast not done a great while, because the
rest of the low countries have made a shift to eat up
thy holland; and God knows whether those that bawl out
the ruins of thy linen shall inherit His kingdom; but
the midwives say the children are not in the fault,
whereupon the world increases and kindreds are mightily
strengthened.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
How ill it follows, after you have labored so hard, you
should talk so idly! Tell me, how many good young
princes would do so, their fathers being so sick as
yours at this time is?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
How ill it follows, after you have labored so hard, you
should talk so idly! Tell me, how many good young
princes would do so, their fathers being so sick as
yours at this time is?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Shall I tell thee one thing, Poins?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Yes, faith, and let it be an excellent good thing.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
It shall serve among wits of no higher breeding than
thine.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Go to. I stand the push of your one thing that you will
tell.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Go to. I stand the push of your one thing that you will
tell.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Marry, I tell thee it is not meet that I should be sad,
now my father is sick —albeit I could tell thee, as to
one it pleases me, for fault of a better, to call my
friend, I could be sad, and sad indeed too.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

now my father is sick —albeit I could tell thee, as to
one it pleases me, for fault of a better, to call my
friend, I could be sad, and sad indeed too.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Very hardly, upon such a subject.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
By this hand, thou thinkest me as far in the devil’s
book as thou and Falstaff for obduracy and persistency.
Let the end try the man. But I tell thee, my heart
bleeds inwardly that my father is so sick: and keeping
such vile company as thou art hath in reason taken from
me all ostentation of sorrow.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: The reason?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What wouldst thou think of me if I should weep?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: I would think thee a most princely hypocrite.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
It would be every man’s thought, and thou art a blessed
fellow to think as every man thinks. Never a man’s
thought in the world keeps the roadway better than
thine. Every man would think me an hypocrite indeed. And
what accites your most worshipful thought to think so?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

what accites your most worshipful thought to think so?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Why, because you have been so lewd and so much
engraffed to Falstaff.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: And to thee.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
By this light, I am well spoke on. I can hear it with
my own ears. The worst that they can say of me is that I
am a second brother, and that I am a proper fellow of
my hands; and those two things, I confess, I cannot
help. By the Mass, here comes Bardolph.

Enter BARDOLPH and the PAGE

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
And the boy that I gave Falstaff. He had him from me
Christian, and look if the fat villain have not
transformed him ape.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
And the boy that I gave Falstaff. He had him from me
Christian, and look if the fat villain have not
transformed him ape.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: God save your Grace.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: And yours, most noble Bardolph.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
[to BARDOLPH] Come, you virtuous ass, you bashful fool,
must you be blushing? Wherefore blush you now? What a
maidenly man-at-arms are you become! Is ’t such a matter
to get a pottle-pot’s maidenhead?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
He calls me e'en now, my lord, through a red lattice,
and I could discern no part of his face from the window.
At last I spied his eyes, and methought he had made two
holes in the ale-wife’s new petticoat and so peeped
through.

sentences.text:

and I could discern no part of his face from the window.
At last I spied his eyes, and methought he had made two
holes in the ale-wife’s new petticoat and so peeped
through.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Has not the boy profited?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Away, you whoreson upright rabbit, away!

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: Away, you rascally Althea’s dream, away!

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Instruct us, boy. What dream, boy?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Instruct us, boy. What dream, boy?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
Marry, my lord, Althea dreamt she was delivered of a
firebrand, and therefore I call him her dream.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
Marry, my lord, Althea dreamt she was delivered of a
firebrand, and therefore I call him her dream.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
A crown’s worth of good interpretation. There ’tis,
boy.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
A crown’s worth of good interpretation. There ’tis,
boy.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
O, that this good blossom could be kept from cankers!
Well, there is sixpence to preserve thee.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
O, that this good blossom could be kept from cankers!
Well, there is sixpence to preserve thee.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
An you do not make him hanged among you, the gallows
shall have wrong.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: And how doth thy master, Bardolph?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Well, my good lord. He heard of your Grace’s coming to
town. There’s a letter for you.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Well, my good lord. He heard of your Grace’s coming to
town. There’s a letter for you.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Well, my good lord. He heard of your Grace’s coming to
town. There’s a letter for you.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Delivered with good respect. And how doth the Martlemas
your master?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Delivered with good respect. And how doth the Martlemas
your master?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: In bodily health, sir.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Marry, the immortal part needs a physician, but that
moves not him. Though that be sick, it dies not.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:

moves not him. Though that be sick, it dies not.

moves not him. Though that be sick, it dies not.

[reads to himself] I do allow this wen to be as
familiar with me as my dog, and he holds his place, for
look you how be writes. [he hands the letter to POINS]

[reads to himself] I do allow this wen to be as
familiar with me as my dog, and he holds his place, for
look you how be writes. [he hands the letter to POINS]

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
[reads] John Falstaff, knight. Every man must know that
as oft as he has occasion to name himself, even like
those that are kin to the King , for they never prick
their finger but they say, “There’s some of the King’s
blood spilt.” “How comes that?” says he that takes upon
him not to conceive. The answer is as ready as a
borrower’s cap: “I am the King’s poor cousin, sir.”

Nay, they will be kin to us, or they will fetch it from

reads]Sir John alstaff, knight, to the son of the King

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Why, this is a certificate.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Peace! [reads] I will imitate the honorable Romans in
brevity.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: He sure means brevity in breath, short-winded.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
[reads] I commend me to thee, I commend thee, and I
leave thee. Be not too familiar with Poins, for he
misuses thy favors so much that he swears thou art to
marry his sister Nell. Repent at idle times as thou
mayest, and so, farewell. Thine by yea and no, which is
as much as to say, as thou usest him, Jack Falstaff with
my familiars, John with my brothers and sisters, and
Sir John with all Europe.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
My lord, I’ll steep this letter in sack and make him
eat it.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
That’s to make him eat twenty of his words. But do you
use me thus, Ned? Must I marry your sister?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
That’s to make him eat twenty of his words. But do you
use me thus, Ned? Must I marry your sister?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
That’s to make him eat twenty of his words. But do you
use me thus, Ned? Must I marry your sister?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
God send the wench no worse fortune! But I never said
so.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
God send the wench no worse fortune! But I never said
so.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Well, thus we play the fools with the time, and the
spirits of the wise sit in the clouds and mock us. [to
BARDOLPH] Is your master here in London?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Yea, my lord.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Where sups he? Doth the old boar feed in the old frank?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Where sups he? Doth the old boar feed in the old frank?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: At the old place, my lord, in Eastcheap.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What company?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: Ephesians, my lord, of the old church.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Sup any women with him?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
None, my lord, but old Mistress Quickly and Mistress
Doll Tearsheet.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What pagan may that be?

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
A proper gentlewoman, sir, and a kinswoman of my
master’s.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Even such kin as the parish heifers are to the town
bull.— Shall we steal upon them, Ned, at supper?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: I am your shadow, my lord. I’ll follow you.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: I am your shadow, my lord. I’ll follow you.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Sirrah—you, boy—and Bardolph, no word to your master
that I am yet come to town. [gives them money] There’s
for your silence.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: I have no tongue, sir.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: And for mine, sir, I will govern it.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: And for mine, sir, I will govern it.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Fare you well. Go.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Fare you well. Go.
Exeunt BARDOLPH and PAGE

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: This Doll Tearsheet should be some road.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
I warrant you, as common as the way between Saint
Alban’s and London.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
How might we see Falstaff bestow himself tonight in his
true colors, and not ourselves be seen?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Put on two leathern jerkins and aprons, and wait upon
him at his table as drawers.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
From a god to a bull: a heavy decension. It was Jove’s
case. From a prince to a 'prentice: a low transformation
that shall be mine, for in everything the purpose must
weigh with the folly. Follow me, Ned.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

case. From a prince to a 'prentice: a low transformation
that shall be mine, for in everything the purpose must
weigh with the folly. Follow me, Ned.

Enter NORTHUMBERLAND, LADY NORTHUMBERLAND, and LADY PERCY

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
I pray thee, loving wife and gentle daughter, Give even
way unto my rough affairs. Put not you on the visage of
the times And be, like them, to Percy troublesome.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
I pray thee, loving wife and gentle daughter, Give even
way unto my rough affairs. Put not you on the visage of
the times And be, like them, to Percy troublesome.

sentences.speaker: LADY NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
I have given over. I will speak no more. Do what you
will; your wisdom be your guide.

sentences.speaker: LADY NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
I have given over. I will speak no more. Do what you
will; your wisdom be your guide.

sentences.speaker: LADY NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
I have given over. I will speak no more. Do what you
will; your wisdom be your guide.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Alas, sweet wife, my honor is at pawn, And, but my
going, nothing can redeem it.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Alas, sweet wife, my honor is at pawn, And, but my
going, nothing can redeem it.

sentences.speaker: LADY PERCY
sentences.text:
O yet, for God’s sake, go not to these wars. The time
was, father, that you broke your word, When you were
more endeared to it than now, When your own Percy, when
my heart’s dear Harry, Threw many a northward look to
see his father Bring up his powers; but he did long in
vain. Who then persuaded you to stay at home? There were
two honors lost, yours and your son’s. For yours, the
God of heaven brighten it. For his, it stuck upon him as
the sun In the gray vault of heaven , and by his light
Did all the chivalry of England move To do brave acts. He
was indeed the glass Wherein the noble youth did dress
themselves. He had no legs that practiced not his gait;
And speaking thick, which nature made his blemish, Became
the accents of the valiant; For those that could speak
low and tardily Would turn their own perfection to abuse
To seem like him. So that in speech, in gait, In diet,
in affections of delight, In military rules, humors of
blood, He was the mark and glass, copy and book, That
fashioned others. And him—O wondrous him! O miracle of
men!—him did you leave, Second to none, unseconded by
you, To look upon the hideous god of war In
disadvantage, to abide a field Where nothing but the
sound of Hotspur’s name Did seem defensible. So you left
him . Never, O never, do his ghost the wrong To hold
your honor more precise and nice With others than with
him. Let them alone. The Marshal and the Archbishop are
strong. Had my sweet Harry had but half their numbers,
Today might I, hanging on Hotspur’s neck, Have talked of
Monmouth’s grave.

more endeared to it than now, When your own Percy, when
my heart’s dear Harry, Threw many a northward look to
see his father Bring up his powers; but he did long in
vain. Who then persuaded you to stay at home? There were
two honors lost, yours and your son’s. For yours, the
God of heaven brighten it. For his, it stuck upon him as
the sun In the gray vault of heaven , and by his light
Did all the chivalry of England move To do brave acts. He
was indeed the glass Wherein the noble youth did dress
themselves. He had no legs that practiced not his gait;
And speaking thick, which nature made his blemish, Became
the accents of the valiant; For those that could speak
low and tardily Would turn their own perfection to abuse
To seem like him. So that in speech, in gait, In diet,
in affections of delight, In military rules, humors of
blood, He was the mark and glass, copy and book, That
fashioned others. And him—O wondrous him! O miracle of
men!—him did you leave, Second to none, unseconded by
you, To look upon the hideous god of war In
disadvantage, to abide a field Where nothing but the
sound of Hotspur’s name Did seem defensible. So you left
him . Never, O never, do his ghost the wrong To hold
your honor more precise and nice With others than with
him. Let them alone. The Marshal and the Archbishop are
strong. Had my sweet Harry had but half their numbers,
Today might I, hanging on Hotspur’s neck, Have talked of
Monmouth’s grave.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Beshrew your heart, Fair daughter, you do draw my
spirits from me With new lamenting ancient oversights.
But I must go and meet with danger there, Or it will seek
me in another place And find me worse provided.

sentences.speaker: LADY NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Oh, fly to Scotland Till that the nobles and the
armèd commons Have of their puissance made a little
taste.

sentences.speaker: LADY PERCY
sentences.text:
If they get ground and vantage of the King, Then join
you with them like a rib of steel To make strength
stronger; but, for all our loves, First let them try
themselves. So did your son; He was so suffered. So came
I a wid ow, And never shall have length of life enough
To rain upon remembrance with mine eyes That it may grow
and sprout as high as heaven For recordation to my noble
husband.

sentences.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
sentences.text:
Come, come, go in with me. 'Tis with my mind As with
the tide swelled up unto his height, That makes a
still-stand, running neither way. Fain would I go to
meet the Archbishop, But many thousand reasons hold me
back. I will resolve for Scotland. There am I Till time
and vantage crave my company.

Enter two DRAWERS

sentences.speaker: FRANCIS
sentences.text:
What the devil hast thou brought there—applejohns? Thou
knowest Sir John cannot endure an applejohn.

sentences.speaker: FRANCIS
sentences.text:
What the devil hast thou brought there—applejohns? Thou
knowest Sir John cannot endure an applejohn.

sentences.speaker: SECOND DRAWER
sentences.text:
Mass, thou sayest true. The Prince once set a dish of
applejohns before him and told him there were five more
Sir Johns and, putting off his hat, said “I will now
take my leave of these six dry, round, old, withered
knights.” It angered him to the heart. But he hath
forgot that.

knights.” It angered him to the heart. But he hath
forgot that.

sentences.speaker: FRANCIS
sentences.text:
Why then, cover, and set them down, and see if thou
canst find out Sneak’s noise. Mistress Tearsheet would
fain hear some music.

sentences.speaker: THIRD DRAWER
sentences.text:
Dispatch: the room where they supped is too hot.
They’ll come in straight.

sentences.speaker: THIRD DRAWER
sentences.text:
Dispatch: the room where they supped is too hot.
They’ll come in straight.

sentences.speaker: FRANCIS
sentences.text:
Sirrah, here will be the Prince and Master Poins anon,
and they will put on two of our jerkins and aprons, and
Sir John must not know of it. Bardolph hath brought
word.

sentences.speaker: THIRD DRAWER
sentences.text:
By the Mass, here will be old utis. It will be an
excellent stratagem.

Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY and DOLL TEARSHEET

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
I' faith, sweetheart, methinks now you are in an
excellent good temperality. Your pulsidge beats as
extraordinarily as heart would desire, and your color, I
warrant you, is as red as any rose, in good truth, la .
But, i' faith, you have drunk too much canaries, and
that’s a marvellous searching wine, and it perfumes the
blood ere one can say “What’s this?” How do you now?

sentences.text:
[sings] When Arthur first in court —Empty the jordan.
[sings] And was a worthy king — How now, Mistress Doll?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: Sick of a calm, yea, good faith.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
So is all her sect. An they be once in a calm, they are
sick.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
So is all her sect. An they be once in a calm, they are

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET

A pox damn you, muddy rascal. Is that all the comfort
you give me?

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
A pox damn you, muddy rascal. Is that all the comfort

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: You make fat rascals, Mistress Doll.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
I make them? Gluttony and diseases make them; I make
them not.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
I make them? Gluttony and diseases make them; I make
them not.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET

the diseases, Doll. We catch of you, Doll, we catch of
you. Grant that, my poor virtue, grant that.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
By my troth, this is the old fashion. You two never
meet but you fall to some discord. You are both, i' good
truth, as rheumatic as two dry toasts. You cannot one
bear with another’s confirmities. What the good-year!
One must bear, and that must be you. You are the weaker
vessel, as they say, the emptier vessel.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Can a weak empty vessel bear such a huge full hogshead?
There’s a whole merchant’s venture of Bourdeaux stuff
in him. You have not seen a hulk better stuffed in the
hold. — Come, I’ll be friends with thee, Jack. Thou art
going to the wars, and whether I shall ever see thee
again or no, there is nobody cares.

Enter FIRST DRAWER

sentences.speaker: FIRST DRAWER
sentences.text: Sir, Ancient Pistol’s below and would speak with you.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Hang him, swaggering rascal! Let him not come hither.
It is the foul-mouthed’st rogue in England.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Hang him, swaggering rascal! Let him not come hither.
It is the foul-mouthed’st rogue in England.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
If he swagger, let him not come here. No, by my faith,
I must live among my neighbors. I’ll no swaggerers: I am
in good name and fame with the very best. S hut the
door. There comes no swaggerers here. I have not lived
all this while to have swaggering now. Shut the door, I
pray you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Dost thou hear, hostess?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Pray you pacify yourself, Sir John. There comes no
swaggerers here.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Pray you pacify yourself, Sir John. There comes no
swaggerers here.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Dost thou hear? It is mine ancient.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Dost thou hear? It is mine ancient.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Tilly-vally, Sir John, ne'er tell me. And your ancient
swaggerer comes not in my doors. I was before Master
Tisick, the debuty t' other day, and, as he said to
me—’twas no longer ago than Wednesday last, i' good
faith— “Neighbour Quickly,” says he—Master Dumb, our
minister, was by then—“Neighbour Quickly,” says he,
“receive those that are civil, for,” said he, “you are
in an ill name.” Now he said so, I can tell whereupon.
“For,” says he, “you are an honest woman, and well
thought on. Therefore take heed what guests you receive.
Receive,” says he, “no swaggering companions.” There
comes none here. You would bless you to hear what he
said. No, I’ll no swaggerers.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
He’s no swaggerer, hostess, a tame cheater, i' faith.
You may stroke him as gently as a puppy greyhound. He’ll
not swagger with a Barbary hen if her feathers turn
back in any show of resistance. —Call him up, drawer.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY

“Cheater,” call you him? I will bar no honest man my
house, nor no cheater, but I do not love swaggering. By
my troth, I am the worse when one says “swagger.” Feel,
masters, how I shake; look you, I warrant you.

no more than will do me good, for no man’s pleasure, I.

Charge me! I scorn you, scurvy companion. What, you
poor, base, rascally, cheating lack-linen mate! Away,
you mouldy rogue, away! I am meat for your master.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Away, you cutpurse rascal, you filthy bung, away! By
this wine, I’ll thrust my knife in your mouldy chaps an
you play the saucy cuttle with me. Away, you bottle-ale
rascal, you basket-hilt stale juggler, you. Since when,
I pray you, sir? God’s light, with two points on your
shoulder? Much!

sentences.text:
God let me not live, but I will murder your ruff for
this.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No more, Pistol. I would not have you go off here.
Discharge yourself of our company, Pistol.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: No, good Captain Pistol, not here, sweet captain.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: No, good Captain Pistol, not here, sweet captain.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Captain? Thou abominable damned cheater, art thou not
ashamed to be called captain? An captains were of my
mind, they would truncheon you out for taking their
names upon you before you have earned them. You a
captain? You slave, for what? For tearing a poor whore’s
ruff in a bawdy house? He a captain! Hang him, rogue.
He lives upon mouldy stewed prunes and dried cakes. A
captain? God’s light, these villains will make the word
as odious as the word “occupy,” which was an excellent
good word before it was ill sorted. Therefore captains
had need look to ’t.

sentences.speaker: PISTOL

Not I. I tell thee what, Corporal Bardolph, I could
tear her. I’ll be revenged of her.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: Pray thee go down.

sentences.speaker: PISTOL
sentences.text:
I’ll see her damned first to Pluto’s damnèd lake, by
this hand, to th' infernal deep with Erebus and tortures
vile also. Hold hook and line, say I. Down, down, dogs!
Down, Fates! Have we not Hiren here?

sentences.speaker: PISTOL
sentences.text:
These be good humors indeed. Shall pack-horses And
hollow pampered jades of Asia, which cannot go but
thirty mile a day, Compare with Caesars and with
cannibals, and Troyant Greeks? Nay, rather damn
them with King Cerberus, and let the welkin roar.
Shall we fall foul for toys?

Then feed and be fat, my fair Calipolis. Come, give ’s
some sack. Si fortune me tormente, sperato me contento.
Fear we broadsides? No, let the fiend give fire. Give me
some sack, and, sweetheart, lie thou there. [lays down
his sword] Come we to full points here? And are
etceteras nothing?

seven stars.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET

For God’s sake, thrust him downstairs. I cannot endure
such a fustian rascal.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET

sentences.speaker: PISTOL
sentences.text: “Thrust him downstairs?" Know we not Galloway nags?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Quoit him down, Bardolph, like a shove-groat shilling.
Nay, an he do nothing but speak nothing, he shall be
nothing here.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF

sentences.speaker: PISTOL
sentences.text:
What! shall we have incision? Shall we imbrue? [
snatches up his sword] Then death rock me asleep, abridge
my doleful days. Why then, let grievous, ghastly,
gaping wounds untwine the Sisters Three. Come, Atropos,
I say.

afore I’ll be in these tirrits and frights. So, murder,
I warrant now. Alas, alas, put up your naked weapons,
put up your naked weapons.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Yea, sir. The rascal’s drunk. You have hurt him, sir,
i' the shoulder.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: A rascal to brave me!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: A rascal to brave me!

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Ah, you sweet little rogue, you. Alas, poor ape, how
thou sweat’st! Come, let me wipe thy face. Come on, you
whoreson chops. Ah, rogue, i' faith, I love thee. Thou
art as valorous as Hector of Troy, worth five of
Agamemnon, and ten times better than the Nine Worthies.
Ah, villain!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Ah, rascally slave! I will toss the rogue in a blanket.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Ah, rascally slave! I will toss the rogue in a blanket.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Do, an thou darest for thy heart. An thou dost, I’ll
canvass thee between a pair of sheets.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
Do, an thou darest for thy heart. An thou dost, I’ll
canvass thee between a pair of sheets.
Enter musicians

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: The music is come, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Let them play.—Play, sirs.—Sit on my knee, Doll. A
rascal bragging slave! The rogue fled from me like
quicksilver.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

quicksilver.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
I' faith, and thou followed’st him like a church. Thou
whoreson little tidy Bartholomew boar-pig , when wilt thou
leave fighting a-days and foining a-nights and begin to
patch up thine old body for heaven?

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:

Enter, behind, PRINCE HENRY and POINS, disguised as drawers

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Peace, good Doll. Do not speak like a death’s-head; do
not bid me remember mine end.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Peace, good Doll. Do not speak like a death’s-head; do
not bid me remember mine end.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: Sirrah, what humor’s the Prince of?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A good shallow young fellow, he would have made a good
pantler; he would a' chipped bread well.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A good shallow young fellow, he would have made a good
pantler; he would a' chipped bread well.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: They say Poins has a good wit.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
He a good wit? Hang him, baboon. His wit’s as thick as
Tewksbury mustard. There’s no more conceit in him than
is in a mallet.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
He a good wit? Hang him, baboon. His wit’s as thick as
Tewksbury mustard. There’s no more conceit in him than
is in a mallet.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: Why does the Prince love him so then?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Because their legs are both of a bigness, and he plays
at quoits well, and eats conger and fennel, and drinks
off candles' ends for flap-dragons, and rides the wild
mare with the boys, and jumps upon joint stools, and
swears with a good grace, and wears his boots very
smooth, like unto the sign of the Leg, and breeds no
bate with telling of discreet stories, and such other
gambol faculties he has that show a weak mind and an
able body, for the which the Prince admits him; for the
Prince himself is such another. The weight of a hair
will turn the scales between their avoirdupois.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
[To POINS] Would not this nave of a wheel have his ears
cut off?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Let’s beat him before his whore.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Look whe'er the withered elder hath not his poll clawed
like a parrot.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Is it not strange that desire should so many years
outlive performance?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Kiss me, Doll.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Saturn and Venus this year in conjunction! What says
th' almanac to that?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Saturn and Venus this year in conjunction! What says
th' almanac to that?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
And look whether the fiery trigon, his man, be not
lisping to his master’s old tables, his notebook, his
counsel keeper.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: [to DOLL] Thou dost give me flattering busses.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: By my troth, I kiss thee with a most constant heart.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: I am old, I am old.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
I love thee better than I love e'er a scurvy young boy
of them all.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What stuff wilt have a kirtle of? I shall receive money
o' Thursday; shalt have a cap tomorrow. A merry song!
Come, it grows late. We’ll to bed. Thou 'lt forget me
when I am gone.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What stuff wilt have a kirtle of? I shall receive money
o' Thursday; shalt have a cap tomorrow. A merry song!
Come, it grows late. We’ll to bed. Thou 'lt forget me
when I am gone.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
By my troth, thou 'lt set me a-weeping an thou sayest
so. Prove that ever I dress myself handsome till thy
return. Well, harken a' th' end.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Some sack, Francis.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY AND POINS
sentences.text: Anon, anon, sir.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY AND POINS
sentences.text: Anon, anon, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Ha? A bastard son of the King’s?—And art not thou Poins
his brother?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Ha? A bastard son of the King’s?—And art not thou Poins
his brother?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Why, thou globe of sinful continents, what a life dost
thou lead?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Why, thou globe of sinful continents, what a life dost
thou lead?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A better than thou. I am a gentleman. Thou art a
drawer.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A better than thou. I am a gentleman. Thou art a
drawer.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Very true, sir, and I come to draw you out by the ears.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
O, the Lord preserve thy good Grace! By my troth,
welcome to London. Now the Lord bless that sweet face of
thine. O Jesu, are you come from Wales?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
O, the Lord preserve thy good Grace! By my troth,
welcome to London. Now the Lord bless that sweet face of
thine. O Jesu, are you come from Wales?

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
O, the Lord preserve thy good Grace! By my troth,
welcome to London. Now the Lord bless that sweet face of
thine. O Jesu, are you come from Wales?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Thou whoreson mad compound of majesty, [indicating
DOLL] by this light flesh and corrupt blood, thou art
welcome.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: How? You fat fool, I scorn you.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: How? You fat fool, I scorn you.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: How? You fat fool, I scorn you.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
My lord, he will drive you out of your revenge and turn
all to a merriment, if you take not the heat.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
My lord, he will drive you out of your revenge and turn
all to a merriment, if you take not the heat.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
You whoreson candle-mine, you how vilely did you speak
of me even now before this honest, virtuous, civil
gentlewoman!

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
God’s blessing of your good heart, and so she is, by my
troth.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
God’s blessing of your good heart, and so she is, by my
troth.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Didst thou hear me?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Yea, and you knew me, as you did when you ran away by
Gad’s Hill. You knew I was at your back, and spoke it on
purpose to try my patience.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Yea, and you knew me, as you did when you ran away by

purpose to try my patience.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No, no, no; not so. I did not think thou wast within
hearing.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No, no, no; not so. I did not think thou wast within
hearing.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I shall drive you, then, to confess the wilfull abuse,
and then I know how to handle you.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

and then I know how to handle you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: No abuse, Hal, o' mine honor, no abuse.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: No abuse, Hal, o' mine honor, no abuse.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Not to dispraise me and call me pantier and
bread-chipper and I know not what?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: No abuse, Hal.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: No abuse?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No abuse, Ned, i' th' world, honest Ned, none. I
dispraised him before the wicked, that the wicked might
not fall in love with thee; in which doing, I have done
the part of a careful friend and a true subject, and thy
father is to give me thanks for it. No abuse, Hal.
—None, Ned, none. No, faith, boys, none.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
See now whether pure fear and entire cowardice doth not
make thee wrong this virtuous gentlewoman to close with
us. Is she of the wicked, is thine hostess here of the
wicked, or is thy boy of the wicked, or honest Bardolph,
whose zeal burns in his nose, of the wicked?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Answer, thou dead elm, answer.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
The fiend hath pricked down Bardolph irrecoverable, and
his face is Lucifer’s privy kitchen, where he doth
nothing but roast malt-worms. For the boy, there is a
good angel about him, but the devil outbids him too.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
The fiend hath pricked down Bardolph irrecoverable, and
his face is Lucifer’s privy kitchen, where he doth
nothing but roast malt-worms. For the boy, there is a
good angel about him, but the devil outbids him too.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: For the women?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
For one of them, she’s in hell already and burns poor
souls. For the other, I owe her money, and whether she
be damned for that I know not.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
For one of them, she’s in hell already and burns poor
souls. For the other, I owe her money, and whether she
be damned for that I know not.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: No, I warrant you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No, I think thou art not; I think thou art quit for
that. Marry, there is another indictment upon thee for
suffering flesh to be eaten in thy house contrary to the
law, for the which I think thou wilt howl.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No, I think thou art not; I think thou art quit for
that. Marry, there is another indictment upon thee for
suffering flesh to be eaten in thy house contrary to the
law, for the which I think thou wilt howl.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No, I think thou art not; I think thou art quit for
that. Marry, there is another indictment upon thee for
suffering flesh to be eaten in thy house contrary to the
law, for the which I think thou wilt howl.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
All vitlars do so. What’s a joint of mutton or two in a
whole Lent?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
All vitlars do so. What’s a joint of mutton or two in a
whole Lent?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: You, gentlewoman.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text: What says your Grace?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: His grace says that which his flesh rebels against.
Knocking within

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Who knocks so loud at door? Look to th' door there,
Francis.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Who knocks so loud at door? Look to th' door there,
Francis.
Enter PETO

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Peto, how now, what news?

sentences.speaker: PETO
sentences.text:
The King your father is at Westminster, And there are
twenty weak and wearied posts Come from the north, and
as I came along I met and overtook a dozen captains,
Bareheaded, sweating, knocking at the taverns And asking
everyone for Sir John Falstaff.

sentences.speaker: PETO
sentences.text:
The King your father is at Westminster, And there are
twenty weak and wearied posts Come from the north, and
as I came along I met and overtook a dozen captains,
Bareheaded, sweating, knocking at the taverns And asking
everyone for Sir John Falstaff.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
By heaven, Poins, I feel me much to blame So idly to
profane the precious time When tempest of commotion,
like the south Borne with black vapour, doth begin to
melt And drop upon our bare unarmèd heads. — Give me my
sword and cloak. —Falstaff, good night.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Now comes in the sweetest morsel of the night, and we
must hence and leave it unpicked.
Knocking within

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: More knocking at the door?
Enter BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: How now, what’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
You must away to court, sir, presently. A dozen
captains stay at door for you.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
You must away to court, sir, presently. A dozen
captains stay at door for you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
[to the PAGE] Pay the musicians, sirrah. —Farewell,
hostess .—Farewell, Doll. You see, my good wenches, how
men of merit are sought after. The undeserver may sleep
when the man of action is called on. Farewell, good
wenches. If I be not sent away post, I will see you again
ere I go.

sentences.text:
I cannot speak. If my heart be not ready to burst—well,
sweet Jack, have a care of thyself.

sentences.speaker: DOLL TEARSHEET
sentences.text:
I cannot speak. If my heart be not ready to burst—well,

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Farewell, farewell.
Exeunt FALSTAFF, BARDOLPH, PAGE, and musicians

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Well, fare thee well. I have known thee these
twenty-nine years, come peascod time, but an honester
and truer-hearted man—well, fare thee well.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: [within] Mistress Tearsheet!

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: [within] Bid Mistress Tearsheet come to my master.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
O, run, Doll, run, run, good Doll. Come.—She comes
blubbered.—Yea! Will you come, Doll?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY

Enter KING Henry in his nightgown, with a page

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Go call the Earls of Surrey and of Warwick; But, ere
they come, bid them o'erread these letters And well
consider of them. Make good speed.

sentences.speaker: KING

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
How many thousand of my poorest subjects Are at this
hour asleep! O sleep, O gentle sleep, Nature’s soft
nurse, how have I frighted thee, That thou no more wilt
weigh my eyelids down And steep my senses in
forgetfulness? Why rather, sleep, liest thou in smoky
cribs, Upon uneasy pallets stretching thee And hushed
with buzzing night-flies to thy slumber, Than in the
perfumed chambers of the great, Under the canopies of
costly state, And lull’d with sound of sweetest melody?
O thou dull god, why liest thou with the vile In
loathsome beds and leavest the kingly couch A watch-case
or a common 'larum bell? Wilt thou upon the high and
giddy mast Seal up the shipboy’s eyes, and rock his
brains In cradle of the rude imperious surge And in the
visitation of the winds, Who take the ruffian billows by
the top, Curling their monstrous heads and hanging them
With deafening clamor in the slippery clouds That with
the hurly death itself awakes? Canst thou, O partial
sleep, give thy repose To the wet sea-boy in an hour so
rude, And, in the calmest and most stillest night, With
all appliances and means to boot, Deny it to a king?
Then, happy low, lie down. Uneasy lies the head that
wears a crown.

Enter WARWICK and SURREY

sentences.speaker: WARWICK
sentences.text: Many good morrows to your Majesty.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text: Is it good morrow, lords?

sentences.speaker: WARWICK
sentences.text: 'Tis one o'clock, and past.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Why then, good morrow to you all, my lords. Have you
read o'er the letter that I sent you?

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Why then, good morrow to you all, my lords. Have you
read o'er the letter that I sent you?

sentences.speaker: WARWICK
sentences.text: We have, my liege.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Then you perceive the body of our kingdom How foul it
is, what rank diseases grow And with what danger near
the heart of it.

sentences.speaker: WARWICK
sentences.text:
It is but as a body yet distempered, Which to his
former strength may be restored With good advice and
little medicine. My Lord Northumberland will soon be
cooled.

sentences.speaker: WARWICK
sentences.text:
It is but as a body yet distempered, Which to his
former strength may be restored With good advice and
little medicine. My Lord Northumberland will soon be
cooled.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
O God, that one might read the book of fate And see the
revolution of the times Make mountains level, and the
continent, Weary of solid firmness, melt itself Into the
sea, and other times to see The beachy girdle of the
ocean Too wide for Neptune’s hips; how chance’s mocks
And changes fill the cup of alteration With divers
liquors! O, if this were seen, The happiest youth,
viewing his progress through, What perils past, what
crosses to ensue, Would shut the book, and sit him down
and die. 'Tis not ten years gone Since Richard and
Northumberland, great friends, Did feast together, and
in two years after Were they at wars. It is but eight
years since This Percy was the man nearest my soul, Who
like a brother toiled in my affairs And laid his love
and life under my foot, Yea, for my sake, even to the
eyes of Richard Gave him defiance. But which of you was
by — [to WARWICK] You, cousin Nevil, as I may remember—
When Richard, with his eye brimful of tears, Then checked
and rated by Northumberland, Did speak these words, now
proved a prophecy? “Northumberland, thou ladder by the
which My cousin Bolingbroke ascends my throne” — Though
then, God knows, I had no such intent, But that
necessity so bowed the state That I and greatness were
compelled to kiss — “The time shall come,” thus did he
follow it, “The time will come that foul sin, gathering
head, Shall break into corruption” —so went on,
Foretelling this same time’s condition And the division
of our amity.

When Richard, with his eye brimful of tears, Then checked
and rated by Northumberland, Did speak these words, now
proved a prophecy? “Northumberland, thou ladder by the
which My cousin Bolingbroke ascends my throne” — Though
then, God knows, I had no such intent, But that
necessity so bowed the state That I and greatness were
compelled to kiss — “The time shall come,” thus did he
follow it, “The time will come that foul sin, gathering
head, Shall break into corruption” —so went on,
Foretelling this same time’s condition And the division
of our amity.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
O God, that one might read the book of fate And see the
revolution of the times Make mountains level, and the
continent, Weary of solid firmness, melt itself Into the
sea, and other times to see The beachy girdle of the
ocean Too wide for Neptune’s hips; how chance’s mocks
And changes fill the cup of alteration With divers
liquors! O, if this were seen, The happiest youth,
viewing his progress through, What perils past, what
crosses to ensue, Would shut the book, and sit him down
and die. 'Tis not ten years gone Since Richard and
Northumberland, great friends, Did feast together, and
in two years after Were they at wars. It is but eight
years since This Percy was the man nearest my soul, Who
like a brother toiled in my affairs And laid his love
and life under my foot, Yea, for my sake, even to the
eyes of Richard Gave him defiance. But which of you was
by — [to WARWICK] You, cousin Nevil, as I may remember—
When Richard, with his eye brimful of tears, Then checked
and rated by Northumberland, Did speak these words, now
proved a prophecy? “Northumberland, thou ladder by the
which My cousin Bolingbroke ascends my throne” — Though
then, God knows, I had no such intent, But that
necessity so bowed the state That I and greatness were
compelled to kiss — “The time shall come,” thus did he
follow it, “The time will come that foul sin, gathering
head, Shall break into corruption” —so went on,
Foretelling this same time’s condition And the division
of our amity.

sentences.speaker: WARWICK
sentences.text:
There is a history in all men’s lives Figuring the
nature of the times deceased, The which observed, a man
may prophesy, With a near aim, of the main chance of
things As yet not come to life, which in their seeds And
weak beginnings lie intreasurèd. Such things become the
hatch and brood of time, And by the necessary form of
this, King Richard might create a perfect guess That
great Northumberland, then false to him, Would of that
seed grow to a greater falseness, Which should not find
a ground to root upon Unless on you.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Are these things then necessities? Then let us meet
them like necessities. And that same word even now cries
out on us. They say the Bishop and Northumberland Are
fifty thousand strong.

sentences.speaker: WARWICK
sentences.text:
It cannot be, my lord. Rumor doth double, like the
voice and echo, The numbers of the feared. Please it
your Grace To go to bed. Upon my soul, my lord, The
powers that you already have sent forth Shall bring this
prize in very easily. To comfort you the more, I have
received A certain instance that Glendower is dead. Your
Majesty hath been this fortnight ill, And these
unseasoned hours perforce must add Unto your sickness.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I will take your counsel. And were these inward wars
once out of hand, We would, dear lords, unto the Holy
Land.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I will take your counsel. And were these inward wars
once out of hand, We would, dear lords, unto the Holy
Land.
Exeunt
Enter Justice SHALLOW and Justice SILENCE, with MOULDY, SHADOW, WART, FEEBLE, BULLCALF, and a servant or two

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Come on, come on, come on. Give me your hand, sir, give
me your hand, sir. An early stirrer, by the rood. And
how doth my good cousin Silence?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Come on, come on, come on. Give me your hand, sir, give
me your hand, sir. An early stirrer, by the rood. And
how doth my good cousin Silence?

sentences.speaker: SILENCE
sentences.text: Good morrow, good cousin Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
And how doth my cousin your bedfellow? And your fairest
daughter and mine, my goddaughter Ellen?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
And how doth my cousin your bedfellow? And your fairest
daughter and mine, my goddaughter Ellen?

sentences.speaker: SILENCE
sentences.text: Alas, a black ousel, cousin Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
By yea and no, sir. I dare say my cousin William is
become a good scholar. He is at Oxford still, is he not?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
By yea and no, sir. I dare say my cousin William is
become a good scholar. He is at Oxford still, is he not?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
By yea and no, sir. I dare say my cousin William is
become a good scholar. He is at Oxford still, is he not?

sentences.speaker: SILENCE
sentences.text: Indeed, sir, to my cost.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
He must then to the Inns o' Court shortly. I was once
of Clement’s Inn, where I think they will talk of mad
Shallow yet.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
He must then to the Inns o' Court shortly. I was once
of Clement’s Inn, where I think they will talk of mad
Shallow yet.

sentences.speaker: SILENCE
sentences.text: You were called “Lusty Shallow” then, cousin.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
By the Mass, I was called anything, and I would have
done anything indeed too, and roundly too. There was I,
and little John Doit of Staffordshire, and black George
Barnes, and Francis Pickbone, and Will Squele, a
Cotswold man. You had not four such swinge-bucklers in
all the Inns o' Court again. And I may say to you, we
knew where the bona robas were and had the best of them
all at commandment. Then was Jack Falstaff, now Sir
John, a boy, and page to Thomas Mowbray, Duke of
Norfolk.

sentences.text:
This Sir John, cousin, that comes hither anon about
soldiers?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
The same Sir John, the very same. I see him break
Scoggin’s head at the court gate, when he was a crack
not thus high; and the very same day did I fight with
one Sampson Stockfish, a fruiterer, behind Grey’s Inn.
Jesu, Jesu, the mad days that I have spent! And to see
how many of my old acquaintance are dead.

Certain, ’tis certain; very sure, very sure. Death, as
the Psalmist saith, is certain to all. All shall die.
How a good yoke of bullocks at Stamford Fair?

Death is certain. Is old Dooble of your town living
yet?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SILENCE
sentences.text: Dead, sir.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Jesu, Jesu, dead! He drew a good bow, and dead? He shot
a fine shoot. John o' Gaunt loved him well, and betted
much money on his head. Dead! He would have clapped i'
th' clout at twelve score, and carried you a forehand
shaft a fourteen and fourteen and a half, that it would
have done a man’s heart good to see. How a score of ewes
now?

Thereafter as they be, a score of good ewes may be

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: And is old Dooble dead?

sentences.speaker: SILENCE
sentences.text: Here come two of Sir John Falstaff’s men, as I think.
Enter BARDOLPH and one with him

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Good morrow, honest gentlemen.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: I beseech you, which is Justice Shallow?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
I am Robert Shallow, sir, a poor esquire of this county
and one of the King’s justices of the peace. What is
your good pleasure with me?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:

and one of the King’s justices of the peace. What is
your good pleasure with me?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
My captain, sir, commends him to you, my captain, Sir
John Falstaff, a tall gentleman, by heaven, and a most
gallant leader.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:

gallant leader.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW

He greets me well, sir. I knew him a good backsword
man. How doth the good knight? May I ask how my lady his
wife doth?

Sir, pardon. A soldier is better accommodated than with
a wife.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Sir, pardon. A soldier is better accommodated than with

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
It is well said, in faith, sir, and it is well said
indeed too. “Better accommodated.” It is good, yea,
indeed, is it. Good phrases are surely, and ever were,
very commendable. “Accommodated.” It comes of accommodo .
Very good, a good phrase.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Pardon, sir; I have heard the word—“phrase” call you
it? By this day, I know not the phrase, but I will
maintain the word with my sword to be a soldierlike
word, and a word of exceeding good command, by heaven.
“Accommodated,” that is when a man is, as they say,
accommodated, or when a man is being whereby he may be
thought to be accommodated, which is an excellent thing.

It is very just. Look, here comes good Sir John. —Give
me your good hand, give me your Worship’s good hand. By
my troth, you like well and bear your years very well.
Welcome, good Sir John.

I am glad to see you well, good Master Robert Shallow.—
Master Sure-card, as I think?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
No, Sir John. It is my cousin Silence, in commission
with me.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
No, Sir John. It is my cousin Silence, in commission

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Good Master Silence, it well befits you should be of
the peace.

sentences.speaker: SILENCE

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Fie, this is hot weather, gentlemen. Have you provided
me here half a dozen sufficient men?

Let me see, let me see, let me see. So, so, so, so, so.

I call, let them do so, let them do so. Let me see,
where is Mouldy?

strong, and of good friends.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Ha, ha, ha, most excellent, i' faith! Things that are
mouldy lack use. Very singular good, in faith. Well
said, Sir John, very well said.

sentences.speaker: MOULDY
sentences.text:
I was pricked well enough before, an you could have let
me alone. My old dame will be undone now for one to do
her husbandry and her drudgery. You need not to have
pricked me. There are other men fitter to go out than I.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Where’s Shadow?

sentences.speaker: SHADOW
sentences.text: Here, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Shadow, whose son art thou?

sentences.speaker: SHADOW
sentences.text: My mother’s son, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Thy mother’s son! Like enough, and thy father’s shadow.
So the son of the female is the shadow of the male. It
is often so, indeed, but much of the father’s substance.

sentences.text: Is thy name Wart?

sentences.speaker: WART
sentences.text: Yea, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Thou art a very ragged wart.

sentences.text: Shall I prick him down, Sir John?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
It were superfluous, for his apparel is built upon his
back, and the whole frame stands upon pins. Prick him no
more.

You may, but if he had been a man’s tailor, he’d ha'
pricked you.—Wilt thou make as many holes in an enemy’s
battle as thou hast done in a woman’s petticoat?

battle as thou hast done in a woman’s petticoat?

sentences.speaker: FEEBLE
sentences.text: I will do my good will, sir. You can have no more.

sentences.speaker: FEEBLE
sentences.text: I will do my good will, sir. You can have no more.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well said, good woman’s tailor, well said, courageous
Feeble. Thou wilt be as valiant as the wrathful dove or
most magnanimous mouse. —Prick the woman’s tailor well,
Master Shallow, deep, Master Shallow.

sentences.text:

Feeble. Thou wilt be as valiant as the wrathful dove or

sentences.speaker: FEEBLE
sentences.text: I would Wart might have gone, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would thou wert a man’s tailor, that thou mightst
mend him and make him fit to go. I cannot put him to a
private soldier that is the leader of so many thousands.
Let that suffice, most forcible Feeble.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would thou wert a man’s tailor, that thou mightst
mend him and make him fit to go. I cannot put him to a
private soldier that is the leader of so many thousands.
Let that suffice, most forcible Feeble.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would thou wert a man’s tailor, that thou mightst
mend him and make him fit to go. I cannot put him to a
private soldier that is the leader of so many thousands.
Let that suffice, most forcible Feeble.

sentences.speaker: FEEBLE
sentences.text: It shall suffice, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: I am bound to thee, reverend Feeble.—Who is next?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Peter Bullcalf o' th' green.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Yea, marry, let’s see Bullcalf.

sentences.speaker: BULLCALF
sentences.text: Here, sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Fore God, a likely fellow. Come, prick me Bullcalf till
he roar again.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Fore God, a likely fellow. Come, prick me Bullcalf till
he roar again.

sentences.speaker: BULLCALF
sentences.text: O Lord, good my lord captain—

sentences.speaker: BULLCALF
sentences.text: O Lord, good my lord captain—

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What, dost thou roar before thou art pricked?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What, dost thou roar before thou art pricked?

sentences.speaker: BULLCALF
sentences.text: O Lord, sir, I am a diseased man.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What disease hast thou?

sentences.speaker: BULLCALF
sentences.text:
A whoreson cold, sir, a cough, sir, which I caught with
ringing in the King’s affairs upon his coronation day,
sir.

sentences.speaker: BULLCALF
sentences.text:
A whoreson cold, sir, a cough, sir, which I caught with
ringing in the King’s affairs upon his coronation day,
sir.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Come, thou shalt go to the wars in a gown. We will have
away thy cold, and I will take such order that my
friends shall ring for thee .— [to SHALLOW ] Is here all?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Come, thou shalt go to the wars in a gown. We will have
away thy cold, and I will take such order that my
friends shall ring for thee .— [to SHALLOW ] Is here all?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Here is two more called than your number. You must have
but four here, sir, and so I pray you go in with me to
dinner.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Here is two more called than your number. You must have
but four here, sir, and so I pray you go in with me to
dinner.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Here is two more called than your number. You must have
but four here, sir, and so I pray you go in with me to
dinner.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Come, I will go drink with you, but I cannot tarry
dinner. I am glad to see you, by my troth, Master
Shallow.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Come, I will go drink with you, but I cannot tarry
dinner. I am glad to see you, by my troth, Master
Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
O, Sir John, do you remember since we lay all night in
the windmill in Saint George’s Field?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: No more of that, good Master Shallow, no more of that.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: No more of that, good Master Shallow, no more of that.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Ha, ’twas a merry night. And is Jane Nightwork alive?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Ha, ’twas a merry night. And is Jane Nightwork alive?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: She lives, Master Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: She never could away with me.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Never, never; she would always say she could not abide
Master Shallow.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Never, never; she would always say she could not abide
Master Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
By the Mass, I could anger her to th' heart. She was
then a bona roba. Doth she hold her own well?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
By the Mass, I could anger her to th' heart. She was
then a bona roba. Doth she hold her own well?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
By the Mass, I could anger her to th' heart. She was
then a bona roba. Doth she hold her own well?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Old, old, Master Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Nay, she must be old. She cannot choose but be old.
Certain, she’s old, and had Robin Nightwork by old
Nightwork before I came to Clement’s Inn.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Nay, she must be old. She cannot choose but be old.
Certain, she’s old, and had Robin Nightwork by old
Nightwork before I came to Clement’s Inn.

sentences.speaker: SILENCE
sentences.text: That’s fifty-five year ago.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Ha, cousin Silence, that thou hadst seen that that this
knight and I have seen!—Ha, Sir John, said I well?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Ha, cousin Silence, that thou hadst seen that that this
knight and I have seen!—Ha, Sir John, said I well?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: We have heard the chimes at midnight, Master Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
That we have, that we have, that we have. In faith, Sir
John, we have. Our watchword was “Hem, boys.” Come,
let’s to dinner; come, let’s to dinner. Jesus, the days
that we have seen! Come, come.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BULLCALF
sentences.text:
Good Master Corporate Bardolph, stand my friend, and
here’s four Harry ten-shillings in French crowns for you.
In very truth, sir, I had as lief be hanged, sir, as
go. And yet, for mine own part, sir, I do not care, but
rather because I am unwilling, and, for mine own part,
have a desire to stay with my friends. Else, sir, I did
not care, for mine own part, so much.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Go to. Stand aside.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Go to. Stand aside.

sentences.speaker: MOULDY
sentences.text:
And, good Master Corporal Captain, for my old dame’s
sake, stand my friend. She has nobody to do anything
about her when I am gone, and she is old and cannot help
herself: You shall have forty, sir.

sentences.speaker: MOULDY
sentences.text:
And, good Master Corporal Captain, for my old dame’s
sake, stand my friend. She has nobody to do anything
about her when I am gone, and she is old and cannot help
herself: You shall have forty, sir.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Go to. Stand aside.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Go to. Stand aside.

sentences.speaker: FEEBLE
sentences.text:
By my troth, I care not. A man can die but once. We owe
God a death. I’ll ne'er bear a base mind. An ’t be my
destiny, so; an ’t be not, so. No man’s too good to
serve ’s prince, and let it go which way it will, he
that dies this year is quit for the next.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Well said. Th' art a good fellow.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Well said. Th' art a good fellow.

sentences.speaker: FEEBLE
sentences.text: Faith, I’ll bear no base mind.

sentences.speaker: FEEBLE
sentences.text: Faith, I’ll bear no base mind.
Enter FALSTAFF, SHALLOW, and SILENCE

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Come, sir, which men shall I have?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Four of which you please.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Sir, a word with you. [aside to FALSTAFF] I have three
pound to free Mouldy and Bullcalf.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Go to, well.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Go to, well.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Come, Sir John, which four will you have?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Do you choose for me.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Marry, then, Mouldy, Bullcalf, Feeble, and Shadow.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Mouldy and Bullcalf! For you, Mouldy, stay at home till
you are past service. —And for your part, Bullcalf, grow
till you come unto it. I will none of you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Mouldy and Bullcalf! For you, Mouldy, stay at home till
you are past service. —And for your part, Bullcalf, grow
till you come unto it. I will none of you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

you are past service. —And for your part, Bullcalf, grow
till you come unto it. I will none of you.

sentences.text:

you are past service. —And for your part, Bullcalf, grow
till you come unto it. I will none of you.
Exeunt MOULDY and BULLCALF

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Sir John, Sir John, do not yourself wrong. They are
your likeliest men, and I would have you served with the
best.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Sir John, Sir John, do not yourself wrong. They are
your likeliest men, and I would have you served with the
best.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Will you tell me, Master Shallow, how to choose a
man?Care I for the limb, the thews, the stature, bulk,
and bigassemblance of a man? Give me the spirit, Master
Shallow.Here’s Wart. You see what a ragged appearance it
is. He shall charge you and discharge you with the
motion of apewterer’s hammer, come off and on swifter
than he thatgibbets on the brewer’s bucket. And this
same half-facedfellow, Shadow, give me this man. He
presents no mark tothe enemy. The foeman may with as
great aim level at theedge of a penknife. And for a
retreat, how swiftly will thisFeeble the woman’s tailor,
run off! O, give me the sparemen, and spare me the
great ones. —Put me a caliver into Wart’s hand,
Bardolph.

than he thatgibbets on the brewer’s bucket. And this
same half-facedfellow, Shadow, give me this man. He
presents no mark tothe enemy. The foeman may with as
great aim level at theedge of a penknife. And for a
retreat, how swiftly will thisFeeble the woman’s tailor,
run off! O, give me the sparemen, and spare me the
great ones. —Put me a caliver into Wart’s hand,
Bardolph.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
He is not his craft’s master. He doth not do it right.
I remember at Mile End Green, when I lay at Clement’s
Inn— I was then Sir Dagonet in Arthur’s show —there was a
little quiver fellow, and he would manage you his piece
thus. And he would about and about, and come you in,
and come you in. “Rah, tah, tah,” would he say.
“Bounce,” would he say, and away again would he go, and
again would he come. I shall ne'er see such a fellow.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
These fellows will do well, Master Shallow. —God keep
you, Master Silence. I will not use many words with you.
Fare you well, gentlemen both. I thank you. I must a
dozen mile to-night. —Bardolph, give the soldiers coats.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Sir John, the Lord bless you. God prosper your affairs.
God send us peace. At your return, visit our house. Let
our old acquaintance be renewed. Peradventure I will
with you to the court.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: On, Bardolph. Lead the men away.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: On, Bardolph. Lead the men away.
Exeunt BARDOLPH and the recruits

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
As I return, I will fetch off these justices. I do see
the bottom of Justice Shallow. Lord, Lord, how subject
we old men are to this vice of lying. This same starved
justice hath done nothing but prate to me of the
wildness of his youth and the feats he hath done about
Turnbull Street, and every third word a lie, duer paid
to the hearer than the Turk’s tribute. I do remember him
at Clement’s Inn, like a man made after supper of a
cheese paring. When he was naked, he was, for all the
world, like a forked radish with a head fantastically
carved upon it with a knife. He was so forlorn that his
dimensions to any thick sight were invincible. He was the
very genius of famine, yet lecherous as a monkey, and
the whores called him “mandrake.” He came ever in the
rearward of the fashion, and sung those tunes to the
overscutched huswives that he heard the carmen whistle,
and swore they were his fancies or his good-nights. And
now is this Vice’s dagger become a squire, and talks as
familiarly of John o' Gaunt as if he had been sworn
brother to him, and I’ll be sworn he ne'er saw him but
once in the tilt-yard, and then he burst his head for
crowding among the Marshal’s men. I saw it and told
John o' Gaunt he beat his own name, for you might have
thrust him and all his apparel into an eel-skin; the
case of a treble hautboy was a mansion for him, a
court. And now has he land and beefs. Well, I’ll be
acquainted with him, if I return, and ’t shall go hard
but I’ll make him a philosopher’s two stones to me. If
the young dace be a bait for the old pike, I see no
reason in the law of nature but I may snap at him. Let
time shape, and there an end.

we old men are to this vice of lying. This same starved
justice hath done nothing but prate to me of the
wildness of his youth and the feats he hath done about
Turnbull Street, and every third word a lie, duer paid
to the hearer than the Turk’s tribute. I do remember him
at Clement’s Inn, like a man made after supper of a
cheese paring. When he was naked, he was, for all the
world, like a forked radish with a head fantastically
carved upon it with a knife. He was so forlorn that his
dimensions to any thick sight were invincible. He was the
very genius of famine, yet lecherous as a monkey, and
the whores called him “mandrake.” He came ever in the
rearward of the fashion, and sung those tunes to the
overscutched huswives that he heard the carmen whistle,
and swore they were his fancies or his good-nights. And
now is this Vice’s dagger become a squire, and talks as
familiarly of John o' Gaunt as if he had been sworn
brother to him, and I’ll be sworn he ne'er saw him but
once in the tilt-yard, and then he burst his head for
crowding among the Marshal’s men. I saw it and told
John o' Gaunt he beat his own name, for you might have
thrust him and all his apparel into an eel-skin; the
case of a treble hautboy was a mansion for him, a
court. And now has he land and beefs. Well, I’ll be
acquainted with him, if I return, and ’t shall go hard
but I’ll make him a philosopher’s two stones to me. If
the young dace be a bait for the old pike, I see no
reason in the law of nature but I may snap at him. Let
time shape, and there an end.
